persian_text: همسال از علم خیزد هم جواب همچنانک خار و گل از خاک و آب
english_translation: Both question and answer arise from knowledge just as the thorn and the rose from earth and water

persian_text: گفت آن خر کاو به شب لاحول خورد جز بدین شیوه نداند راه کرد
english_translation: He replied The ass that ate Lá hawl during the night cannot get along except in this manner

persian_text: آن تقاضای دو چشم دل شناس کاو همیجوید ضیای بیقیاس
english_translation: Recognise that that is the craving of the eyes of your heart which is seeking the immeasurable Light

persian_text: هر رسول شاه باید جنس او آب و گل کو خالق افلاک کو
english_translation: Every kings messenger must be of his kind where are water and clay in comparison with the Creator of the heavens

persian_text: ای برادر چون ببینی قصر او چون که در چشم دلت رسته ست مو
english_translation: O brother how wilt thou behold his palace when hair has grown in the eye of thy heart

persian_text: مهر مومش حاکی انگشتری است باز آن نقش نگین حاکی کیست
english_translation: The seal impressed on his wax is telling of the sealring of whom again does the device tell graven on the stone of the ring

persian_text: چون جوالی بس گرانی میبری زان نباید کم که در وی بنگری
english_translation: When you are carrying a very heavy sack you must not fail to look into it

persian_text: گفت صد خدمت کنم پانصد سجود بندهای دارم تن اسپید و جهود
english_translation: He replied I will perform a hundred services and five hundred prostrations  I have a handsome slave but a Jew

persian_text: رزق جویی را ز بالا خو گرم تو ز بالا بر گشودستی درم
english_translation: I am accustomed to seeking daily bread from above Thou hast opened to me the door from above

persian_text: تا به گاه مرگ حصنی باشدش که نیابد خصم راه مقصدش
english_translation: In order that in the hour of death she should have a stronghold which the Enemy would find no way to attack

persian_text: روحهایی کز قفسها رستهاند انبیای رهبر شایستهاند
english_translation: The spirits which have escaped from their cages are the prophets worthy guides

persian_text: نار خندان باغ را خندان کند صحبت مردانت از مردان کند
english_translation: The laughing pomegranate makes the garden laughing  companionship with men makes you one of the men

persian_text: گفتگر بودی ورا دل یا جگر کی بدینجا آمدی بار دگر
english_translation: He replied If he had possessed a heart or liver how should he have come here a second time

persian_text: ما محب و صادق و دل خستهایم در دو عالم دل به تو در بستهایم
english_translation: We are loving and true and with wounded hearts in the two worlds we have fixed our hearts on thee

persian_text: همچو رنجوری و همچون خواب و درد جان ازین اوصاف باشد پاک و فرد
english_translation: Such as sickness and sleep and pain the spirit is untouched by these affections and wholly detached

persian_text: دانشی دیگر ز نادانی ما سر برآوردی عیان که انی انا
english_translation: From our nescience another knowledge would rise into clear view saying Lo I am God

persian_text: عتاب کردن آتش را آن پادشاه جهود
english_translation: How the fire reproached the Jewish king

persian_text: بلک از آن مستان که چون میمیخورند عقلهای پخته حسرت میبرند
english_translation: Nay be one of those inebriates on account of whom whilst they are drinking the wine  mature intellects suffer regret

persian_text: سر امسینا لکردیا بدان راز اصبحنا عرابیا بخوان
english_translation: Know the secret of In the evening I was a Kurd read the mystery of In the morning I was an Arab

persian_text: پای تو در گل مرا گل گشته گل مر ترا ماتم مرا سور و دهل
english_translation: Your feet are in the mud to me the mud has become roses You have mourning I have feasting and drums

persian_text: چون محک آمد بلا و بیم جان زان پدید آید شجاع از هر جبان
english_translation: Tribulation and fear for ones life are like a touchstone thereby the brave man is distinguished from every coward

persian_text: همچنین هر روز تیر انداختی لیک جای گنج را نشناختی
english_translation: Every day in like fashion he was shooting arrows but never getting to know the situation of the treasure

persian_text: اندر آن صحرا که آن اشتر شتافت اشتر خود نیز آن دیگر بیافت
english_translation: In the desert whither that camel had hastened the other one also found his own camel

persian_text: واندرین یم ماهیان پر فناند مار را از سحر ماهی میکنند
english_translation: And in this Sea are artful fish by magic turn snakes into fishes

persian_text: این بهار نو ز بعد برگریز هست برهان وجود رستخیز
english_translation: This new springtide after the fall of the leaves is a proof of the existence of the Resurrection

persian_text: چون سپاه رنج آمد بست دم خود نمیگوید ترا من دیدهام
english_translation: When the army of Pain arrives she holds her breath she will not even say I have seen thee

persian_text: چونک روحانی بود خود چون بود فتنهاش هر لحظه دیگرگون بود
english_translation: When it is spiritual how must it be Its fascination changes at every moment

persian_text: آن خطا دیدن ز ضعف عقل اوست عقل کل مغز است و عقل جزو پوست
english_translation: The view that it is faulty arises from the weakness of his understanding Universal Reason is the kernel and the particular reason is the rind

persian_text: مومنی باشم سلامتجوی من زانک این هر دو بود حظ بدن
english_translation: I am a believer seeking salvation for both these are concerned with the body

persian_text: حکایت پاسبان کی خاموش کرد تا دزدان رخت تاجران بردند به کلی بعد از آن هیهای و پاسبانی میکرد
english_translation: Story of the watchman who kept silence till the robbers had carried off the entire stock of the merchants but afterwards made an outcry and did the duty of a watchman

persian_text: زانک او سنگ سیه بد این عقیق آن عدوی نور بود و این عشیق
english_translation: For that one was a black stone this one a cornelian that one was an enemy to the Light and this one passionately enamoured

persian_text: سغبهی صورت شد آن خواجهی سلیم که به ده میشد بگفتاری سقیم
english_translation: That simple Khwája was duped by form for he was going to the country on infirm words

persian_text: جان سپر کن تیغ بگذارای پسر هر که بیسر بود از این شه برد سر
english_translation: Make your soul a shield and drop the sword O son whoever is headless saves his head from this King

persian_text: من نخسپم حارسی رز کنمگر بر آرد گرگ سر تیرش زنم
english_translation: I will not sleep I will guard the vines if the wolf raise his head I will shoot the arrow at him

persian_text: میکشاندشان موکل سوی شهر میبرد از کافرستانشان به قهر
english_translation: Their custodian is marching them along to the city he is taking them by force from the land of the infidels

persian_text: گر وظیفه بایدت ره پاککن هین بیا و دفع آن بیباک کن
english_translation: If thou want the allowance clear the way hey come on and repel that irreverent one

persian_text: گفت پیغمبر که از بهر مهان حق نجس را پاک گرداند بدان
english_translation: The Prophet said Know that God makes impure pure for the great

persian_text: اعور آن باشد که حالی دید و بس چون بهایم بیخبر از بازپس
english_translation: The oneeyed man is he who saw only the presentignorant like the beasts of after

persian_text: ای برادر بود اندر ما مضی شهریی با روستایی آشنا
english_translation: In the past O brother there was a townsman intimate with a countryman

persian_text: شاه را در خانهی بیذق نهد اینچنین باشد عطا که احمق دهد
english_translation: He puts the king in the house of the pawn the gifts which a fool makes are like this

persian_text: مرغک آمد سوی او از ناشناخت پس طوافی کرد و پیش مرد تاخت
english_translation: A little bird approached him in ignorance  then it hopped round and ran up to the man

persian_text: گرچه این کلک قلم خود بیحسیست نیست جنس کاتب او را مونسیست
english_translation: Although this reedpen is in fact an insensible thing and is not homogeneous with the writer it is a familiar friend to him

persian_text: از چهی بنموده معدومی خیال تا در اندازد اسودا کالجبال
english_translation: Something nonexistent causes a phantom to appear in a well in order that it may cast into it lions as mountains

persian_text: پس توانم که همه حلوا خورم چون کنم صبری صبورم لاجرم
english_translation: Therefore when I deny myself once I can eat halwá entirely of course I am very selfdenying

persian_text: قبله کردم من همه عمر از حول آن خیالاتی که گم شد در اجل
english_translation: through seeing double did I make the lifelong object of my attention those phantoms that vanished at the fated hour

persian_text: همچو ابلیسی که میگفتای سلام رب أنظرنی إلی یوم القیام
english_translation: like the Devil who was saying O Preserver O my Lord grant me a respite till the day of Resurrection

persian_text: که شفای جمله رنجوران شد آب از همایونی وحی مستطاب
english_translation: So that its water became a cure for all the sick by the blessedness of the goodly revelation

persian_text: مثل نبود آن مثال آن بود تا برد بوی آنک او حیران بود
english_translation: Tis not a comparison tis a parable thereof in order that he who is bewildered may get a scent

persian_text: پیش تو که بس گرانست و جماد مطربست او پیش داود اوستاد
english_translation: To you the mountain is exceedingly heavy and inanimate to David it is a mastermusician

persian_text: ای شتر که تو مثال مومنی کم فتی در رو و کم بینی زنی
english_translation: O camel thou who art a type of the true believer dost not fall on thy face nor dost thou turn up thy nose

persian_text: نی خدا را امتحانی میکنند نی در سود و زیانی میزنند
english_translation: Neither do they put God to any test nor do they knock at the door of any profit or loss

persian_text: آن مگر سلطان بود شاه رفیع یا بود مقبول سلطان و شفیع
english_translation: That friend in sooth may be the Sultan and exalted King or he may be one accepted of the Sultan and one who intercedes

persian_text: چون در افکندش بجست و جوی کار بعد از آن در بست که کابین بیار
english_translation: When He has plunged him into search for the matter  after that He shuts the door saying Bring the dowry

persian_text: وعدهی مهمانیاش را منکری پس ز مطبخ خاک و خاکستر بری
english_translation: Dost thou disbelieve in the promise of being His guest Then from the kitchen thou wilt get dust and ashes

persian_text: پس بگفتندش به اقبال تو شاه غالب آییم و شود کارش تباه
english_translation: Then they said to him Through thy fortune O King we shall prevail and his cause shall be ruined

persian_text: چون نبد بو جهل از اصحاب درد دید صد شق قمر باور نکرد
english_translation: since Bú Jahl was not of those in sympathy  he saw a hundred cleavings of the moon and believed not

persian_text: در زمان پیش آید آن دوزخ گلو حجتش اینکه خدا گفتا کلوا
english_translation: That hellthroat at once comes forward this his argument that God has said Eat ye

persian_text: چشم من پرست و سیرست و غنی از دو صد خورشید دارد روشنی
english_translation: Saying My eye is full and satisfied and wanting naught it is illumined by two hundred suns

persian_text: گویدش جنت گذر کن همچو باد ورنه گردد هر چه من دارم کساد
english_translation: Paradise says to him Pass like the wind or else all that I possess will become unsalable

persian_text: چون سلیمان شو که تا دیوان تو سنگ برند از پی ایوان تو
english_translation: Become like Solomon in order that thy demons may hew stone for thy palace

persian_text: هدیه بردن عرب سبوی آب باران از میان بادیه سوی بغداد
english_translation: How the Arab carried a jug of rainwater from the midst of the desert as a gift to the Commander of the Faithful at Baghdád

persian_text: بر سرت ریزیم ما بهر جزا که شهیدیم آمده اندر غزا
english_translation: We pour on thy head in repayment for we are martyrs come to war

persian_text: گفت قابیل آه شه بر عقل من که بود زاغی ز من افزون به فن
english_translation: Qábíl cried Oh fie on my intellect for a crow is superior to me in skill

persian_text: ای دلیلت گندهتر پیش لبیب در حقیقت از دلیل آن طبیب
english_translation: O you whose evidence in the eyes of the Sage is really more stinking than the evidence of the physician

persian_text: روبها این دم حیلت را بهل وقف کن دل بر خداوندان دل
english_translation: O fox abandon this tail contrivance and devote your heart to the lords of the heart

persian_text: اندرین جو آنچ بر بالاست هست خواه بالا خواه در وی دار دست
english_translation: That which is above is in this River take possession of it either above or in that as you please

persian_text: زانک بر بانگ دهل در سال تنگ جمعه را کردند باطل بیدرنگ
english_translation: Because in a year of distress  at the sound of the drum they without tarrying made void the Friday congregation

persian_text: چونک پرید از دهانش حمد حق مرغ جنت ساختش رب الفلق
english_translation: When praise of God has flown from his mouth the Lord of the daybreak fashions it into a bird of Paradise

persian_text: بود شاهی در زمانی پیش از این ملک دنیا بودش و هم ملک دین
english_translation: In olden time there was a king to whom belonged the power temporal and also the power spiritual

persian_text: چون دلش آموخت شمع افروختن آفتاب او را نیارد سوختن
english_translation: Since his heart has learned to light the candle  the sun cannot burn him

persian_text: ترک خندیدن گرفت از داستان چشم تنگش گشت بسته آن زمان
english_translation: The Turk began to laugh at the stories and at that moment his narrow eyes closed

persian_text: قصدشان تفریق اصحاب رسول فضل حق را کی شناسد هر فضول
english_translation: Their aim is to cause disunion amongst the Companions of the Prophet how should any vain fool understand the grace of God

persian_text: خود نباشد آفتابی را دلیل جز که نور آفتاب مستطیل
english_translation: Verily there is no evidence for a sun except the light of the lofty sun

persian_text: گر بر آن حالت ترا بودی بقا کی رسیدی مر ترا این ارتقا
english_translation: If thou hadst remained in that condition how should this height have been reached by thee

persian_text: یوسفی شد در جمال و در دلال گفتش اکنون رو بده وا گوی حال
english_translation: He became a Joseph in beauty and in coquetry he said to him Now go home and relate what has befallen thee

persian_text: بردران ای دل تو ایشان را مهایست پوستشان بر کن کشان جز پوست نیست
english_translation: O my heart tear them to pieces do not lag rend their skins for they have naught but skin

persian_text: لفظها و نامها چون دامهاست لفظ شیرین ریگ آب عمر ماست
english_translation: Words and names are like pitfalls the sweet word is the sand for the water of our life

persian_text: عشقورزی آن دریچه کردنست کز جمال دوست سینه روشنست
english_translation: The business of love is to make that window  for the breast is illumined by the beauty of the Beloved

persian_text: ما نه مرغان هوا نه خانگی دانهی ما دانهی بیدانگی
english_translation: We are neither birds of the air nor domestic  our grain is the grain of grainlessness

persian_text: نام او در نامها مکتوم کرد محرمان را سر آن معلوم کرد
english_translation: She concealed his name in names and made the inner meaning thereof known to confidants

persian_text: چونک استادند پیش تخت شاه یار شبشان بود آن شاه چو ماه
english_translation: When they stood before the Kings throne that moonlike King was their nights companion

persian_text: گفت و الله عالم السر الخفی کافرید از خاک آدم را صفی
english_translation: He said  by God who knows the thought most deeply hid who out of dust created Adam pure

persian_text: بلک جملهی ماهیان در موجها جملهی پرندگان بر اوجها
english_translation: Nay all the fish in the waves  all the birds in the lofty regions

persian_text: گر دم خلع و مبارا میرود بد مبین ذکر بخارا میرود
english_translation: If talk of khul and mubárá is going on  do not disapprove mention is being made of Bukhárá

persian_text: تا سلیمان لسین معنوی در نیاید بر نخیزد این دوی
english_translation: Until the spiritual Solomon skilled in tongues shall intervene this duality will not disappear

persian_text: شیر و گرگ و خرس و هر گور و دده گرد بر گرد تو شب گرد آمده
english_translation: Lions and wolves and bears and onagers and wild animals gather around thee by night

persian_text: مکیان را عز یکی بد صد شده تا قیامت عزشان ممتد شده
english_translation: the glory of the Meccans had been one it became a hundred their glory was now extending to the Resurrection

persian_text: نان ز خوکان و سگان نبود دریغ کسپ مردم نیست این باران و میغ
english_translation: bread is not withheld from pigs and dogs this rain and clouds are not earned by Man

persian_text: تا در این ظلمت تحری گسترند هر کسی رو جانبی میآورند
english_translation: In order that in this darkness they may spread endeavour they are turning every one their faces in some direction

persian_text: باز بر موجود افسونی چو خواند زو دو اسبه در عدم موجود راند
english_translation: When again He recited a spell over the existent at His word the existent marched posthaste into nonexistence

persian_text: اشترا تنگ گلی بر پشت تست کز نسیمش در تو صد گلزار رست
english_translation: O camel on thy back is a bale of roses from the perfume of which a hundred rosaries grew within thee

persian_text: در دل عاشق به جز معشوق نیست در میانشان فارق و فاروق نیست
english_translation: In the lovers heart is naught but the beloved there is nothing to separate and divide them

persian_text: نور هر گوهر کز او تابان شدی حق و باطل را از او فرقان شدی
english_translation: the light that shone from every pearl became a criterion for distinguishing between truth and falsehood

persian_text: گر مسی گردد ز گفتارت نفیر کیمیا را هیچ از وی وام گیر
english_translation: If a copper turn away in repugnance from your saying  by no means withhold the elixir from him

persian_text: گفت عاشق دوست میجوید بتفت چونک معشوق آمد آن عاشق برفت
english_translation: He said The lover hotly seeks the beloved when the beloved comes the lover is gone

persian_text: عیب کم گو بندهی الله را متهم کم کن به دزدی شاه را
english_translation: Do not find fault with the Servant of God do not suspect the King of being a thief

persian_text: باز شب منسوخ شد از نور روز تا جمادی سوخت ز آن آتش فروز
english_translation: Again night is cancelled by the light of day so that the inanimateness is consumed by that firekindling one

persian_text: حالتی دیگر بود کان نادر است تو مشو منکر که حق بس قادر است
english_translation: There is another state  which is rare do not thou disbelieve for God is very mighty

persian_text: همچو فرعونی که موسی هشته بود طفلکان خلق را سر میربود
english_translation: You are like Pharaoh who had left Moses and was taking off the heads of the peoples babes

persian_text: جهل آید پیش او دانش شود جهل شد علمی که در ناقص رود
english_translation: If ignorance come to him  it becomes knowledge the knowledge that goes into the disbelieving man becomes ignorance

persian_text: خر نخواندت اسپ خواندت ذوالجلال اسپ تازی را عرب گوید تعال
english_translation: The Almighty did not call you an ass He called you a horse the Arabs say to the Arab horse taál

persian_text: در تو نمرودی است آتش در مرو رفت خواهی اول ابراهیم شو
english_translation: In thee is a Nimrod do not go into the fire If thou wish to go in first become Abraham

persian_text: اندکی صرفه بکن از خواب و خور ارمغان بهر ملاقاتش ببر
english_translation: Refrain thyself a little from sleep and food bring the gift for thy meeting with Him

persian_text: هست آن پیدا به پیش چشم دل جهد کن سوی دل آ جهد المقل
english_translation: that is manifest to the eye of the heart  exert thyself advance towards the heart with the exertion of one whose means are small

persian_text: بس بجوشیدی درین عهد مدید ترکجوشی هم نگشتی ای قدید
english_translation: Thou hast been boiling mightily during this long time  O dried meat thou hast not even become halfcooked

persian_text: پس فراموشش شود چون رفت پیش کار او زان آه و زاریهای خویش
english_translation: So when its business has gone forward  it forgets its sighs and lamentations

persian_text: مومنان در حشر گویند ای ملک نی که دوزخ بود راه مشترک
english_translation: At the Gathering the true believers will say O angel is it not that Hell is the common road

persian_text: آن ستون را دفن کرد اندر زمین تا چو مردم حشر گردد یوم دین
english_translation: He buried that pillar in the earth that it may be raised from the dead like mankind on the day of Resurrection

persian_text: پس کی داند راه گلشنهای او پس کی داند جای گلخنهای او
english_translation: Then who should know the way to His roseries Then who should know the way to His furnaces

persian_text: البقیه البقیهای خدیو تا نگردد شاد کلی جان دیو
english_translation: the remnant the remnant O Sovereign that the soul of the Devil may not rejoice entirely

persian_text: خلعت طاووس آید ز آسمان کی رسی از رنگ و دعویها بدان
english_translation: The peacocks garment of honour comes from Heaven how wilt thou attain thereto by means of colours and pretences

persian_text: ور مرادت مال و زر و گوهرست این ز ملک شهر خود آسانترست
english_translation: And if your object is riches and gold and jewels this is even more easy than to take possession of the city

persian_text: تو مرا باور مکن کز آفتاب صبر دارم من و یا ماهی ز آب
english_translation: Do not believe of me that I can endure to be without the Sun or the fish to be without water

persian_text: در عذاب منکرست آن جان او گزدم غم دل دل غمدان او
english_translation: His soul is in hateful torment the scorpion of grief is in his griefladen heart

persian_text: گرچه در ایمان و دین ناموقنم لیک در ایمان او بس مومنم
english_translation: Although I feel no certainty as regards the Faith and Religion  yet I believe mightily in his Faith

persian_text: یک کنیزک دید شه بر شاهراه شد غلام آن کنیزک جان شاه
english_translation: On the kings highway the king espied a handmaiden the king was enthralled by her

persian_text: آن دغلکاری و دزدیهای او و آن چو فرعونان انا و انای او
english_translation: his rascalities and thieveries and Pharaohlike expressions of selfglorification

persian_text: گفت ای ابله چه میگویی مرا من گرفته بودم آخر مر و را
english_translation: He answered O fool what are you telling me Why I had caught him

persian_text: گفت ایازای مهتران نامور امر شه بهتر به قیمت یا گهر
english_translation: Ayáz said O renowned princes is the Kings command more precious or the pearl

persian_text: لیک خادع گشته و مانع شد ز جست ز آب شیرینی کزو صد سبزه رست
english_translation: Yet it beguiles and prevents from seeking the sweet water by which a hundred plants are made to grow

persian_text: کل شیء قاله غیر المفیق إن تکلف أو تصلف لا یلیق
english_translation: Everything that is said by one who has not returned to consciousness if he constrains himself or boastfully exaggerates is unseemly

persian_text: در نماز این خوش اشارتها ببین تا بدانی کین بخواهد شد یقین
english_translation: Behold in the ritual prayer these goodly indications in order that you may know these will certainly come to pass

persian_text: چونک از هستی خود او دور شد منتهای کار او محمود بد
english_translation: because he was far removed from his existence the end of his affair was praiseworthy

persian_text: یک درم خواهی تو امروزای شهم یا که فردا چاشتگاهی سه درم
english_translation: Would you like one dirhem today my king or three dirhems at breakfasttime tomorrow

persian_text: در یکی گفته که بگذار آن خود کان قبول طبع تو ردست و بد
english_translation: In one he said Let go all that belongs to self for it is wrong and bad to comply with your nature

persian_text: انفقوا گفتست پس کسپی بکن زانک نبود خرج بیدخل کهن
english_translation: He hath said Spend therefore earn something since there can be no expenditure without an old income

persian_text: گفت اگر من نیستم اسرار خوان هم تو بر خوان نام را بر استخوان
english_translation: He said If I am not one to pronounce mysteries do thou pronounce the Name over the bones

persian_text: ما عیال حضرتیم و شیر خواه گفت الخلق عیال للإله
english_translation: We are the family of the Lord and craving after milk  he said The people are Gods family

persian_text: عین آتش در اثیر آمد یقین پرتو و سایهی ویست اندر زمین
english_translation: Assuredly the essence of fire is in the aether on the earth there is its ray and shadow

persian_text: از پی این عیش و عشرت ساختن صد هزاران جان بشاید باختن
english_translation: It behoves  for the sake of procuring delight and enjoyment to gamble away hundreds of thousands of souls

persian_text: گر انیس لا نهای ای جان به سر در کمین لا چرایی منتظر
english_translation: O soul if you are not inwardly congenial to nonexistence why are you waiting in ambush for nonexistence

persian_text: چشمبسته عقل جسته مولهه مست ترک مدعی از قهقهه
english_translation: His eyes shut his reason flown bewildered the boastful Turk was intoxicated with guffaws

persian_text: عشق نان مرده را میجان کند جان که فانی بود جاویدان کند
english_translation: Love makes the dead bread into spirit it makes the spirit that was perishable everlasting

persian_text: حلق ببریده خورد شربت ولی حلق از لا رسته مرده در بلی
english_translation: The throat that has been cut drinks sherbet but the throat that has been delivered from Nay and has died in Yea

persian_text: چون شنیدی بعضی اوصاف بلال بشنو اکنون قصهی ضعف هلال
english_translation: Since you have heard some of the qualities of Bilál now hear the story of the emaciation of Hilál

persian_text: ما صباح آییم پیش اوستا تا ببینیم اصل این مکر شما
english_translation: In the morning we will come to the master that we may see the bottom of this trick of yours

persian_text: گفت موسی آن سوم ملک دوتو دو جهانی خالص از خصم و عدو
english_translation: Moses said This third is a twofold empire appertaining to the two worlds  free from adversary and enemy

persian_text: ز آنکه موسی را تو مه رو کردهای ماه جانم را سیهرو کردهای
english_translation: By that whereby Thou hast made Moses face like the moon Thou hast made the moon of my soul to be blackfaced

persian_text: گر بدیدی ز آینه او یک پشیز بیخیالی زو نماندی هیچچیز
english_translation: If he saw in the mirror a single mite without any phantasy nothing would be left of him

persian_text: نقش ظاهر بهر نقش غایبست وان برای غایب دیگر ببست
english_translation: The external form is for the sake of the unseen form and that took shape for the sake of another unseen

persian_text: آن مثل آوردن آن حضرتست که بعلم سر و جهر او آیتست
english_translation: That use of similitudes belongs to the Lord for He is the authority for the knowledge of the hidden and the manifest

persian_text: اژدها را دار در برف فراق هین مکش او را به خورشید عراق
english_translation: Keep the dragon in the snow of separation  beware do not carry it into the sun of Iráq

persian_text: ذرهای از عقل و هوش ار با منست این چه سودا و پریشان گفتنست
english_translation: If a single mote of reason and consciousness is with me what is this melancholy madness and distracted speech

persian_text: ذکر با او همچو سبزه گلخن است بر سر مبرز گلست و سوسن است
english_translation: In relation to him praise of God is the herbage of the ashpit it is roses and lilies upon a dunghill

persian_text: گفت پس چون رحم داری بر همه همچو چوپانی به گرد این رمه
english_translation: She said Then since thou hast pity on all and art like the shepherd around this flock

persian_text: اشکمش گفتی جواب بیطنین که اباد الله کید الکاذبین
english_translation: His belly would say in soundless response May God destroy the plots of the liars

persian_text: اندر آن بستان اگر خندیدهای تو گل بستان جان و دیدهای
english_translation: If thou hast laughed in that garden thou art the rose of the garden of the spirit and the eye

persian_text: که ببرم دست و پاتان از خلاف پس در آویزم ندارمتان معاف
english_translation: Saying I will cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides then I will hang you up I will not hold you exempt

persian_text: هین بگو نک روز من پیروز شد آنچ فردا خواست شد امروز شد
english_translation: Take heed and say Lo my fortune is triumphant that which would have happened tomorrow has happened today

persian_text: پس غدای عاشقان آمد سماع که درو باشد خیال اجتماع
english_translation: Therefore samá is the food of lovers  since therein is the phantasy of composure

persian_text: آید از خواجه ره افکندگی ناید از بنده بغیر بندگی
english_translation: From the master comes the practice of selfabasement from the slave comes naught but slavishness

persian_text: ور نمیدانی شدن زین آستان باری از من گوش کن این داستان
english_translation: And if you cannot become of this threshold  at least hear from me the following tale

persian_text: همچو آتش کرد مقراضی برون میبرید و لب پر افسانه و فسون
english_translation: In a flash he whipped out a pair of scissors and went on cutting while his lips were full of tales and beguiling talk

persian_text: دست میزند بهر منعش بر دهان چند گویی پیش دانای نهان
english_translation: He was putting his hand on his mouth to prevent him  How long wilt thou speak in the presence of one who knows the occult

persian_text: زین خرد جاهل همی باید شدن دست در دیوانگی باید زدن
english_translation: It behoves us to become ignorant of this wisdom must we clutch at madness

persian_text: آن من بر چرخ و آن او نشیب زانک قرب حق برونست از حساب
english_translation: Mine was up to heaven and his was down below  because nighness unto God is beyond calculation

persian_text: همچو آن مستی که پرد بر اثیر مه کنارش گیرد و گوید که گیر
english_translation: Like the man intoxicated who flies to heaven the Moon embraces him and says Embrace

persian_text: مر کلوخ کعبه را چون قبله کرد خاک مردان باشای جان در نبرد
english_translation: Inasmuch as he made the clods of the Kaba the qibla  do thou O soul be the dust of holy men in war

persian_text: گفت موسی هین تو دانی رو رسید نطق این هر دو شود بر تو پدید
english_translation: Hark said Moses thou knowest  Go it has arrived the language of both of these will be revealed to thee

persian_text: با عوام این جمله بسته و مردهای زین عجبتر من ندیدم پردهای
english_translation: To the vulgar all this is a bound and dead  I have not seen a veil more wonderful than this

persian_text: چون امیران از حسد جوشان شدند عاقبت بر شاه خود طعنه زدند
english_translation: When the Amírs boiled over with envy  at last they taunted their King

persian_text: از بهشت انداختش بر روی خاک چون سمک در شست او شد از سماک
english_translation: He cast him from Paradise upon the face of the earth he fell from Simák into his net like a fish

persian_text: کین مریدان من و من آب شور میخورند از من همی گردند کور
english_translation: saying These are my disciples and I am briny water they drink of me and become blind

persian_text: پیش خلقان خوار و زار و ریشخند پیش حق محبوب و مطلوب و پسند
english_translation: In the sight of the people contemptible and wretched and a laughingstock in Gods sight loved and sought and approved

persian_text: این چگونه شمعها افروختست کین دو دیدهی خلق ازینها دوختست
english_translation: What kind of candles are these He hath lighted so that the eyes of His creatures are screened from them

persian_text: باش تا اجزای تو چون بیضهها مرغها زایند اندر انتها
english_translation: Wait till your limbs like eggs hatch birds ultimately

persian_text: حاش لله از کمال جاه او کابر بیماری بپوشد ماه او
english_translation: God forbid in view of the perfection of his attainments that the cloud of sickness should cover his moon

persian_text: هر که جوید پادشاهی و ظفر کم نیاید لقمهی نانای پسر
english_translation: Whoever seeks sovereignty and victory a mouthful of bread will not fail  O son

persian_text: دیدهی غیبت چو غیبست اوستاد کم مبادا زین جهان این دید و داد
english_translation: Thine eye which beholds the invisible is a master like the Invisible may this vision and gift not vanish from this world

persian_text: یا مگر ابری شود فانی راه تا نگردد او حجاب روی ماه
english_translation: Unless a cloud becomes naughted in the Way in order that it may not be a veil to the face of the Moon

persian_text: جمله مرغان ترک کرده چیک چیک همزبان و یار داود ملیک
english_translation: All the birds left off chirping and joined their voices with King David as accompanists

persian_text: پاسخش دادند که خدمت کنیمگر تو خواهی یک مه اینجا ساکنیم
english_translation: They answered him saying We will do service  if thou desire we will dwell here a month

persian_text: آلت زرگر به دست کفشگر همچو دانهی کشت کرده ریگ در
english_translation: The goldsmiths tool in the hand of a shoemaker is like a seed sown in sand

persian_text: آن یکی در چشم تو باشد چو مار هم وی اندر چشم آن دیگر نگار
english_translation: That same one in your eyes is like a snake is a picture in the eyes of another

persian_text: پس فقیر آن شیخ را احوال گفت عذر را با آن غرامت کرد جفت
english_translation: Then the dervish told the Shaykh how the case stood and coupled excuses with the discharge of that obligation

persian_text: حضرتش گفتی کهای صدر مهین این چه عشقست و چه استسقاست این
english_translation: The Lord would say to him O most noble prince what passion is this and what unquenchable thirst is this

persian_text: من بدانم کو فرستاد آن بمن از ضمیر چون سهیل اندر یمن
english_translation: I know that he has sent it to me from the depths of a soul like Canopus in Yemen

persian_text: این حیاتی خفیه در نقش ممات وان مماتی خفیه در قشر حیات
english_translation: This is a life concealed in the form of death while that is a death concealed in the husk of life

persian_text: چون ز فهم این عجایب کودنیگر بلی گویی تکلف میکنی
english_translation: Forasmuch as you are too dull to apprehend these wonders  if you say yea you will be prevaricating

persian_text: گر چه گفتی نیست سر گفت هست هین ببالا پر مپر چون جغد پست
english_translation: Albeit there is no speech there is the inmost soul of speech Come fly aloft do not fly low like the owl

persian_text: من همیرانم شما را همچو مست از در افتادن در آتش با دو دست
english_translation: I as a drunken man drive you away with both hands from falling into the fire

persian_text: گر ترا قندی دهد آن زهر دانگر به تن لطفی کند آن قهر دان
english_translation: If he give thee some candy regard it as poison if he do a kindness to thy body regard it as cruelty

persian_text: هر گرانی و کسل خود از تنست جان ز خفت جمله در پریدنست
english_translation: Every state of heaviness and indolence indeed is from the body the spirit from its lightness  is all on the wing

persian_text: باز فرمان میرسد بردار سر از رکوع و پاسخ حق بر شمر
english_translation: Then comes the command Lift up thy head from the genuflexion and tell over answer to God

persian_text: آب را بستود و او تایق نبود رخ درید و جامه او عاشق نبود
english_translation: He praised the water but he had no longing  he tore his face and raiment but he was no lover

persian_text: اول هر آدمی خود صورتست بعد از آن جان کو جمال سیرتست
english_translation: In sooth the first of every human being is the form after that the spirit which is beauty of disposition

persian_text: تهمتی بر ما منهای سختجان که نهایم آمن ز مکر دشمنان
english_translation: Do not lay any suspicion upon us O man of valiant spirit for we are not secure from the craft of enemies

persian_text: آنچ کردی با من از احسان و بر بندهی تو گشتهام من مستمر
english_translation: the benefit and kindness that thou hast done to me I have become thy slave perpetually

persian_text: در روش یمشی مکبا خود چرا چون همیشاید شدن در استوا
english_translation: Why indeed walks falling in wayfaring when it behoves him to walk in an upright posture

persian_text: عاقبت دیدن نباشد دستباف ور نه کی بودی ز دینها اختلاف
english_translation: To foresee the end is not a handloom otherwise how would there have been difference in religions

persian_text: گر بدیدی حس حیوان شاه را پس بدیدی گاو و خر الله را
english_translation: If the animal sense could see the King  then the ox and the ass would behold Allah

persian_text: هر که آنجا بگذرد زر میبرد نیست هدیهی مخلصان را مسترد
english_translation: every one who passes may carry gold away the gifts of the sincere are never taken back

persian_text: حضرت پر رحمت است و پر کرم عاشق او هم وجود و هم عدم
english_translation: He is the merciful and bountiful Lord both existence and nonexistence are in love with Him

persian_text: گر شود پر نور روزن یا سرا تو مدان روشن مگر خورشید را
english_translation: If the window or the house is full of light do not deem aught luminous except the sun

persian_text: مه جمادست و بود شرقش جماد جان جان جان بود شرق فاد
english_translation: The moon is inanimate and its east is inanimate the hearts east is the soul of the soul of Soul

persian_text: من نیارم رو ز فرمان تافتن خصم خود را رو بیاور سوی من
english_translation: I dare not avert my face from the command Go bring thy adversary before me

persian_text: تاجری بر در نهد لجم سیاه تا شود تاریک مرج و سبزهگاه
english_translation: A trader puts black loam on the pearl so that the meadow and verdant ground becomes dark

persian_text: گفت یا ربا چه پنهان گوهریای غریب عرش چونی خوشتری
english_translation: O Lord he cried what a hidden pearl art thou How art thou O heavenly stranger Art thou better

persian_text: سبطیند این قوم و گوسالهپرست در چنین گاوی چه میمالند دست
english_translation: These folk are Israelites and worshippers of the calf why do they fondle such a cow

persian_text: با چنان قوت که او را بود هم موج آن ملکش فرومیبست دم
english_translation: Even with such strength as he had the waves of that kingdom were stifling his breath

persian_text: جو فرو بر مشک آباندیش را تا گران بینی تو مشک خویش را
english_translation: Carry down to the river the waterskin that has thoughts of the water so that you may find your waterskin heavy

persian_text: کرم در بیخ درخت تن فتاد بایدش بر کند و در آتش نهاد
english_translation: The worm has entered the root of the bodys tree it must be dug up and put in the fire

persian_text: من نگویم زین طریق آید مراد میطپم تا از کجا خواهد گشاد
english_translation: I do not assert that my object will be gained in this way I am palpitating to ascertain from what quarter it will appear

persian_text: چونک او مبدل شدست و شادیش نیست زین زندان و زین آزادیش
english_translation: Since he has been transmuted and his joy is not caused by this prison and this freedom of his

persian_text: چون شکست او بال آن رای نخست چون نشد هستی بالاشکن درست
english_translation: Inasmuch as He broke the wings of that first intention  how was not the existence of the Wingbreaker perfectly established

persian_text: حال هر روزی بدی مانند نی همچو جو اندر روش کش بند نی
english_translation: Each days state is not like the day before as a rive that hath no obstacle in its course

persian_text: لیک در خواهم ز نیکوداوری تا که ایمان آن زمان با خود بری
english_translation: But I will crave of good dispensation that thou mayst take the Faith away with thee at that time

persian_text: و آن دگر بس تیزگوش و سخت کر گنج و در وی نیست یک جو سنگ زر
english_translation: And the second was very sharp of hearing and extremely deafa treasure in which there is not a barleycorns weight of gold

persian_text: سنگ و آهن خود سبب آمد و لیک تو به بالاتر نگرای مرد نیک
english_translation: The stone and the iron are indeed causes but look higher O good man

persian_text: این چه ژاژست و چه هرزهای فلان من حقیقت یافتم چه بود نشان
english_translation: What silly gabble and nonsense is this O fellow I found the reality what is the clue

persian_text: یاد یاران یار را میمون بود خاصه کان لیلی و این مجنون بود
english_translation: Happy it is for a friend to be remembered by friends in particular when that is Laylá and this Majnún

persian_text: اهل آن عالم چو آن عالم ز بر تا ابد در عهد و پیمان مستمر
english_translation: The people of that world like that world on account of probity continue for ever in covenant and promise

persian_text: ناگهان آوازهی پیگار شد تازیان را وقت زین و کار شد
english_translation: Suddenly came the rumour of war twas the time for the Arab horses to be saddled and brought into action

persian_text: چون غم خود نیست این بیمار را چون غم جان نیست این مردار را
english_translation: How has this sick man no care for himself How has this corpse no care for life

persian_text: عاشق آن عاشقان غیب باش عاشقان پنج روزه کم تراش
english_translation: Be the lover of those unseen lovers do not cherish the lovers who last five days

persian_text: گفت میری مر مرا حق داده است سروری و امر مطلق داده است
english_translation: He replied God hath given the Amírate to me He hath given me the chief authority and the absolute command

persian_text: گفت روزی حاکمش ای وعده کژ پیش آ در کار ما وا پس مغژ
english_translation: One day the Governor said to him O false promiser go forward with my affair do not creep back

persian_text: پس که هدم مسجد ما بیگمان نبود الا بعد مرگ ما بدان
english_translation: Know then that without doubt the ruin of our mosque does not occur except after our death

persian_text: او ز خشم آمد گریبانش گرفت چند مشتی زد به رویش ناشکفت
english_translation: He came angrily and seized his collar having lost patience he struck him in the face with his fist several times

persian_text: تو چه دانی تا ننوشی قالشان زانک پنهانست بر تو حالشان
english_translation: Until you hear their words what should you know  inasmuch as their state is hidden from you

persian_text: شمس هم معدهی زمین را گرم کرد تا زمین باقی حدثها را بخورد
english_translation: The sun also warms the belly of the earth so that the earth consumes the remaining ordures

persian_text: بحر کاو آبی به هر جو میدهد هر خسی را بر سر و رو مینهد
english_translation: The ocean which gives some water to every stream lays on its head and face every piece of rubbish

persian_text: عقل جزوی عقل را بدنام کرد کام دنیا مرد را بیکام کرد
english_translation: The particular intelligence has given the intelligence a bad name worldly desire has deprived the man of his desire

persian_text: که چنین اندیشی از بهر ملوک کیف تلقی الرزق ان لم یرزقوک
english_translation: Thou thinkest so concerning the kings how shouldst thou meet the daily provision unless they provide thee

persian_text: در پی خورشید وحی آن مه دوان وآن صحابه در پیش چون اختران
english_translation: The Moon was running behind the Sun of inspiration while the Companions followed behind him like the stars

persian_text: بعد از آن درمان خشمش چون کنم زنده خود را زین مگر مدفون کنم
english_translation: How should I heal his anger after that Surely by this I should bury myself alive

persian_text: شرفهای بشنید در شب معتمد برگرفت آتشزنه که آتش زند
english_translation: A man of trust heard a sound of footsteps during the night he took up the firelighter to strike a flame

persian_text: چون سرش ببرید شد سوی قصاب تا اهابش بر کند در دم شتاب
english_translation: After he had cut off her head he went to the butcher in order that he might quickly rip off her hide forthwith

persian_text: گفت حقشای پیمبر فاش گو غدر را ور جنگ باشد باش گو
english_translation: God said to him O Prophet proclaim the excuse  and if war be  say Let it be

persian_text: بر نشست او نه پشت خر سزد پشت تابوتیش اولیتر سزد
english_translation: The back of an ass is not his proper seat the back of a bier is more fitting for him

persian_text: جواب قاضی سال صوفی را و قصهی ترک و درزی را مثل آوردن
english_translation: The Cadis answer to the questions of the Súfí and how he adduced the Story of the Turk and the Tailor as a parable

persian_text: وقت ذکر غزو شمشیرش دراز وقت کر و فر تیغش چون پیاز
english_translation: At the time when war is spoken Of his scimitar is long  at the time of combat his sword is like an onion

persian_text: تا به بینی نایدت از غیب بو غیر بینی هیچ میبینی بگو
english_translation: Until the scent from the Unseen shall come to your nose say will you see anything except your nose

persian_text: روبهانه باشد آن صید مرید مرده گیرد صید کفتار مرید
english_translation: The prey caught by the disciple is after the manner of the fox the froward hyena catches prey dead

persian_text: تشنگیش از نام او ساکن شدی نام یوسف شربت باطن شدی
english_translation: Her thirst would be quenched by his name the name of Joseph was a sherbet to her soul

persian_text: عیسی و ادریس بر گردون شدند با ملایک چونک همجنس آمدند
english_translation: Jesus and Idrís ascended to heaven since they were homogeneous with the angels

persian_text: خشم خود بشکن تو مشکن تیر را چشم خشمت خون شمارد شیر را
english_translation: Break your own anger do not break the arrow the eye of your anger reckons milk blood

persian_text: خاطرش شد سوی صوفی قنق اندر آن سودا فرو شد تا عنق
english_translation: His thoughts have turned to the Súfí guest he is sunk up to the neck in that business

persian_text: اینچنین مستیست ز استدراج حق تا چه مستیها کند معراج حق
english_translation: Such intoxication arises from Gods gradual temptation so that what intoxications are wrought by the ascension to God

persian_text: زیر لب میگفتی از بیم عدو کوری او بر مناره رو بگو
english_translation: From fear of the foe thou wert wont to speak under thy breath  to his confusion go up into the minaret and speak

persian_text: آمد او آن جا و از دور ایستاد مر عمر را دید و در لرز اوفتاد
english_translation: He came thither and stood afar off he saw Umar and fell atrembling

persian_text: و انکه سر تا پا گل است و سوسن است پس بهار او را دو چشم روشن است
english_translation: While to one that is roses and lilies from head to foot Spring is a pair of bright eyes

persian_text: دل نگه دارید ای بیحاصلان در حضور حضرت صاحب دلان
english_translation: Keep watch over your hearts O fruitless ones in the presence of the majesty of the men of heart

persian_text: بقیهی قصهی مطرب و پیغام رسانیدن عمر به او آن چه هاتف آواز داد
english_translation: The rest of the story of the minstrel and how the Commander of the Faithful Umar may God be wellpleased with him conveyed to him the message spoken by the heavenly voice

persian_text: عشق و سودا چونک پر بودش بدن مینبودش چاره از بیخود شدن
english_translation: Since his body was full of love and passion he had no resource but to become beside himself

persian_text: این کند از امر و او بهر ستیز بر سر استیزه رویان خاک ریز
english_translation: This one acts by the command  and he for the sake of quarrelling  Pour dust on the heads of those who have quarrelsome faces

persian_text: ز انکه مخلص در خطر باشد ز دام تا ز خود خالص نگردد او تمام
english_translation: For the mukhlis is in danger of the snare until he becomes entirely purged of self

persian_text: در عموم تاویل این آیت کی کلما اوقدوا نارا للحرب
english_translation: Concerning the interpretation in a general sense of the Verse as often as they kindle a fire for war

persian_text: قصهی آن گنجنامه کی پهلوی قبهای روی به قبله کن و تیر در کمان نه بینداز آنجا کی افتد گنجست
english_translation: Story of the treasurescroll  Beside a certain domed building turn your face towards the qibla and put an arrow to the bow and shoot the treasure is at the spot where it falls

persian_text: چون شود جانش محک نقدها پس ببیند قلب را و قلب را
english_translation: When his soul becomes the touchstone of the coin then he will see the heart and the false money

persian_text: این سزای آنک بیتدبیر عقل بانگ غولی آمدش بگزید نقل
english_translation: This is what is deserved by him who the cry of a ghoul came to him without rational foresight chose to move

persian_text: تا تو ندهی هیچکس ندهد یقینای گشاینده تو بگشا بند این
english_translation: Unless Thou give assuredly no one will give  O Opener do Thou open the lock of this

persian_text: هیچ در گوش کسی زیشان نرفت کین طمع آمد حجاب ژرف و زفت
english_translation: Naught entered into the ear of a single one of them for this desire is a barrier huge and stout

persian_text: ما سمعیعیم و بصیریم و خوشیم با شما نامحرمان ما خامشیم
english_translation: We have hearing and sight and are happy with you the uninitiated we are mute

persian_text: حکایت نذر کردن سگان هر زمستان کی این تابستان چون بیاید خانه سازیم از بهر زمستان را
english_translation: Story of the vow made by the dogs every winter that when next summer comes they will build a house for the winter

persian_text: جای روح پاک علیین بود کرم باشد کش وطن سرگین بود
english_translation: The abode of the pure spirit is Illiyyín tis the worm that has its home in dung

persian_text: شمع چون دعوت کند وقت فروز جان پروانه نپرهیزد ز سوز
english_translation: When the candle calls at the moment of illumination the soul of the moth does not shrink from burning

persian_text: حق نه قادر بود بر خلق فلک در یکی لحظه به کن بیهیچ شک
english_translation: Was not God able to create heaven in one moment by Be Without any doubt

persian_text: چون ببینی مشک پر مکر و مجاز لب ببند و خویشتن را خنب ساز
english_translation: when you see a waterskin full of deceit and falsehood shut your lips and make yourself like a jar

persian_text: ما هم از وی اعجمی سازیم خویش پاسخش آریم چون بیگانه پیش
english_translation: Let us too feign ourselves to be ignorant thereof and elicit the answer to it as strangers

persian_text: هین ز گنج رحمت بیمر بده در کف تو خاک گردد زر بده
english_translation: Hark give from the incalculable treasure of mercy in thy hand earth will become gold give

persian_text: حاصل این آمد که بد کنای کریم با لیمان تا نهد گردن لیم
english_translation: The upshot is thisO noble man do evil to the vile that the villain may lay his neck

persian_text: هر که بستاند ترا دشنام ده سود و سرمایه به مفلس وامده
english_translation: Revile any one that praises you lend interest and capital to the destitute

persian_text: این سبب را محرم آمد عقلها و آن سببها راست محرم انبیا
english_translation: minds are familiar with this cause but the prophets are familiar with those causes

persian_text: تخت او سیار بیحمال شد حلقه و در مطرب و قوال شد
english_translation: Its throne moved along without carrier its doorring and door became musician and singer

persian_text: وقت تنگ و میرود آب فراخ پیش از آن کز هجر گردی شاخ شاخ
english_translation: The time is restricted and the abundant water is flowing away ere through being parted  you fall to pieces

persian_text: خورده گندم حله زو بیرون شده خلد بر وی بادیه و هامون شده
english_translation: He had eaten the wheat  his celestial robe had been stripped off him and Paradise had become for him a desert and sandy plain

persian_text: گفت پیغمبر عداوت از خرد بهتر از مهری که از جاهل رسد
english_translation: The Prophet said Enmity from wisdom is better than the love that comes from a fool

persian_text: ور ز غیر صورتت نبود فره صورتی کان بیتو زاید در تو به
english_translation: And if you derive no advantage except from form the form that comes to birth within you involuntarily is the best

persian_text: حاصل آن آمد حقیقت نام ما پیش حضرت کان بود انجام ما
english_translation: In brief that which is our end is really our name with God

persian_text: ای بلال افراز بانگ سلسلت ز آن دمی کاندر دمیدم در دلت
english_translation: O Bilál lift up thy mellifluous voice from that breath which I breathed into thy heart

persian_text: چشمهای نیک هم بر وی به دست از ره غیرت که حسنش بیحدست
english_translation: Even the good eyes are evil to him in respect of jealousy for his beauty is infinite

persian_text: گفت دور از دولت و از شاهیت که درآید غصه در آگاهیت
english_translation: He said Far be it from thy empire and kingship that anxiety should enter into thy consciousness

persian_text: گر چه چون نشفش کند تو قادری کش از ایشان واستانی واخری
english_translation: Although when they absorb it Thou art able to take it back from them and redeem it

persian_text: بعد چندین سال میزاید شهی میزند بر آسمانها خرگهی
english_translation: After such and such a number of years a king will be born  he will pitch a tent above the heavens

persian_text: منگر اندر نقش زشت و خوب خویش بنگر اندر عشق و در مطلوب خویش
english_translation: Do not regard thy ugly or beauteous form regard Love and the object of thy search

persian_text: باز رستم من ز تشویش و عذاب دوش خوش خفتم در آن بیخوف خواب
english_translation: I was delivered from anxiety and torment last night I slept sound in a peaceful sleep

persian_text: رابعا چون سوخت ما را مزرعه میندانم خامسه از رابعه
english_translation: Fourthly forasmuch as my cornfield is burntup I do not know the fifth from the fourth

persian_text: مفلس است این و ندارد هیچچیز قرض تا ندهد کس او را یک پشیز
english_translation: This man is insolvent and has nothing let no one lend him a single brass farthing

persian_text: همره خورشید را شبپر مخوان آنک او مسجود شد ساجد مدان
english_translation: Do not call the fellowtraveller of the Sun a bat do not call him who was worshipped a worshipper

persian_text: راه ده آلودگان را العجل در فرات عفو و عین مغتسل
english_translation: Speedily grant the defiled ones admission into the Euphrates of pardon and the fountain a washingplace

persian_text: این خموشی مرکب چوبین بود بحریان را خامشی تلقین بود
english_translation: The wooden horse is this silence silence gives instruction to the seafolk

persian_text: اتفاق افتاد کان روز ورود موسی اندر زیر نخلی خفته بود
english_translation: It chanced that on the day of their arrival Moses was asleep under a palmtree

persian_text: ذکر نفس عادیان کالت بیافت در قتال انبیا مو میشکافت
english_translation: an account of the carnal soul of people like Ád which found weapons took the utmost pains to combat the prophets

persian_text: بوی پیغامبر ببرد آن شیر نر همچنانک بوی یوسف را پدر
english_translation: That fierce lion scented the Prophet just as the scent of Joseph was perceived by his father

persian_text: تو بگو دادهی خدا بهتر بود یاکه لنگی راهوارانه رود
english_translation: Tell  is the gift of God better or that a lame person should walk smoothly

persian_text: کو نبی وقت خویشست ای مرید تا ازو نور نبی آید پدید
english_translation: Who is the prophet of his own time O disciple so that the Light of the Prophet is manifested by him

persian_text: زان عوان سر شدی دزد و تباه تا عوانان را به قهر تست راه
english_translation: On account of that inward myrmidon you have become thievish and depraved so that the way is for the myrmidons to coerce you

persian_text: شعله در بنگاه انسانی زنیم خار را گلزار روحانی کنیم
english_translation: We will set fire to the tenement of Man and make the thorns a spiritual garden of roses

persian_text: گر نبینی آب کورانه بفن سوی جو آور سبو در جوی زن
english_translation: If you do not see the water artfully after the fashion of the blind bring the jug to the river and dip it in the river

persian_text: ز ان گدا رویان نادیده ز آز آن در رحمت بر ایشان شد فراز
english_translation: Because of those impudent wretches who were blinded by greed that gate of mercy was closed upon them

persian_text: کارت این بودست از وقت ولاد صید مردم کردن از دام وداد
english_translation: This has been thy business from the hour of birth to catch people with the trap of love

persian_text: خواندن بیدرد از افسردگیست خواندن با درد از دلبردگیست
english_translation: The call of the griefless is from a frozen heart the call of the grieving one is from rapture

persian_text: صبر کن با فقر و بگذار این ملال ز آنکه در فقر است عز ذو الجلال
english_translation: Have patience with poverty and abandon this disgust because in poverty there is the majesty of the Lord of glory

persian_text: آتش ظلم و فساد افروختن مسجد و سجدهکنان را سوختن
english_translation: To kindle the fire of iniquity and corruption to burn the mosque and those who bend low in prayer

persian_text: عارفان از دو جهان کاهلترند زانک بی شد یار خرمن میبرند
english_translation: The gnostics are the laziest folk in the two worlds because they get their harvest without ploughing

persian_text: چون عتاب اهبطوا انگیختند همچو هاروتش نگون آویختند
english_translation: When they uttered the rebuke Get ye down they suspended it head first like Hárút

persian_text: جز نفخت کان ز وهاب آمدست روح را باش آن دگرها بیهدست
english_translation: except I breathed for that hath come from the Munificent Cleave to the spirit The other things are vain

persian_text: میشکنجد حور دستش میکشد کور حیران کز چه دردم میکند
english_translation: The houri is nipping his hand and drawing  the blind man is distraught and says Wherefore is he hurting me

persian_text: حقتعالی فخر آورد از وفا گفت من اوفی بعهد غیرنا
english_translation: The high God hath boasted of faithfulness He hath said Who but I am most faithful in keeping a promise

persian_text: هین بیا که من رسولم دعوتی چون اجل شهوتکشم نه شهوتی
english_translation: Hark come for I am a Messenger sent to call  like Death I am the slayer of lust I am not given to lust

persian_text: نیست باشد طعم خل چون میچشی هست اوقیه فزون چون برکشی
english_translation: The flavour of the vinegar when you taste  is nonexistent the ounce exists surplus when you weigh

persian_text: تو بپرس از هر که خواهی این خبر تا بگوید بی شکنجه بیضرر
english_translation: Ask whomsoever thou wilt for information about this so that he may tell without torture and without harm

persian_text: در دلم زین خنده ظنی اوفتاد راستی گو عشوه نتوانیم داد
english_translation: From this laughter a suspicion has come into my heart tell the truth thou canst not cajole me

persian_text: این دعا میخواهد او از عام و خاص کالخلاص و الخلاص و الخلاص
english_translation: He is begging this prayer of high and low crying Release and release and release

persian_text: توبه را از جانب مغرب دری باز باشد تا قیامت بر وری
english_translation: From the quarter of the West a door of repentance is open to mankind till the Resurrection

persian_text: کار او از جادویگر گشت زفت جادوی کردیم ما هم چون نرفت
english_translation: If his cause became mighty by sorcery we too practised sorcery how did not it succeed

persian_text: بد محالی جست کاو دنیا بجست نیک حالی جست کاو عقبی بجست
english_translation: An evil resort sought he that sought this world a good state sought he that sought the world to come

persian_text: من نخواهم زد دگر از خوف و بیم اینچنین طبل هوا زیر گلیم
english_translation: Nevermore in fear and dread will I beat such a drum of passion under a blanket

persian_text: فرش سوزان سردی از جالس برد فرش افسرده حرارت را خورد
english_translation: A burning carpet takes away coldness from one who sits  a frozen carpet consumes heat

persian_text: گر ازین انبار خواهی بر و بر نیمساعت هم ز همدردان مبر
english_translation: If you desire benefit and wheat from this Barn do not part even for half an hour from those who sympathise

persian_text: قلعه را چون آب آید از برون در زمان امن باشد بر فزون
english_translation: When water comes to a fortress from outside it is more than enough in times of peace

persian_text: عرق جنسیت چو جنبیدن گرفت سر به زانو بر نهادند از شگفت
english_translation: When the vein of homogeneity began to throb they laid their heads upon their knees in astonishment

persian_text: چه حلالای گشته از اهل ضلال غیر خون تو نمیبینم حلال
english_translation: How lawful O thou that hast become one of the lost I deem nothing lawful but thy blood

persian_text: تا نماز دیگر آن کودک گریست شیخ دیده بست و در وی ننگریست
english_translation: Till afternoon prayers the boy wept the Shaykh closed his eyes and did not look at him

persian_text: نادر اکسیری که از وی نیم تاب بر ظلامی زد به گردش آفتاب
english_translation: The unique elixir of which half a gleam struck upon a darkness and made it the sun

persian_text: خواب دیدم خواجهی معطی المنی واحد کالالف ان امر عنی
english_translation: In my dream I saw the Khwája the giver of things desired one man like a thousand if any affair happened

persian_text: ماجرا بسیار شد در من یزید داد صد دینار و آن از وی خرید
english_translation: The altercation in bidding was prolonged he paid a hundred dinars and bought it from him

persian_text: تو ملاف از مشک کان بوی پیاز از دم تو میکند مکشوف راز
english_translation: Do not brag of musk for that smell of onions is revealing the secret of your breath

persian_text: تا گشاید دخمه کان بر نیستیست تا بیاید آن نسیم عیش و زیست
english_translation: in order that the charnelhouse which is on notbeing might open and that he might feel the breeze of vitality and life

persian_text: مینبینی صنع ربانیت را که کشید او موی پیشانیت را
english_translation: Are not you beholding the action of your Lord who dragged you by the forelock

persian_text: صرصری بر عاد قتالی شده مر سلیمان را چو حمالی شده
english_translation: A roaring wind became murderous to Ád to Solomon it became as a carrier

persian_text: بو دوای چشم باشد نور ساز شد ز بویی دیدهی یعقوب باز
english_translation: The scent is a remedy for the eye lightmaking the eye of Jacob was opened by a scent

persian_text: لافکیشی کاسهلیسی طبلخوار بانگ طبلش رفته اطراف دیار
english_translation: A braggart a lickplatter a parasite the noise of his drum has reached the remotest parts of the world

persian_text: مرغ بیهنگام و راه بیرهی آتشی پر در بن دیگ تهی
english_translation: a cock that crows at the wrong time a road that leads nowhere a big fire beneath an empty kettle

persian_text: رحمت تو وآن دم گیرای تو پر شود این عالم از احیای تو
english_translation: From thy bringing the dead to life this world is filled with thy mercy and with that potent breath of thine

persian_text: گفت لاحولای پدر لاحول کن با رسول اهل کمتر گو سخن
english_translation: Good gracious cried he implore Gods grace O father and say little to a messenger who knows his business

persian_text: نرد بس نادر ز رحمت باخته عین کفران را انابت ساخته
english_translation: He in His mercy has played a very marvellous game of backgammon He has made the essence of ingratitude to be a turning in repentance

persian_text: تو نبینی برگها را کف زدن گوش دل باید نه این گوش بدن
english_translation: You do not see the clapping of the leaves one must have the spiritual ear not this ear of the body

persian_text: حزم آن باشد که چون دعوت کنند تو نگویی مست و خواهان منند
english_translation: Prudence is this that when they invite you you should not say They are enamoured and fond of me

persian_text: ظلمت اشکال زان جوید دلش تا که افزونتر نماید حاصلش
english_translation: His heart seeks the darkness of perplexity in order that his acquirements may seem greater

persian_text: فرق زشت و نغز از عقل آورید نی ز چشمی کز سیه گفت و سپید
english_translation: Get the distinction between evil and good from reason not from the eye that tells of black and white

persian_text: اولش دو دو به آخر لت بخور جز درین ویرانه نبود مرگ خر
english_translation: The beginning thereof is daw daw  in the end lat khwar  the death of the ass is not except in this wilderness

persian_text: کاین سبو پر زر به دست او دهید چون که واگردد سوی دجلهش برید
english_translation: Saying Give into his hand this jug full of gold When he returns  take him to the Tigris

persian_text: صلح کن با این پدر عاقی بهل تا که فرش زر نماید آب و گل
english_translation: Make peace with this Father abandon disobedience that the water and clay may appear a carpet of gold

persian_text: لفظ چون وکرست و معنی طایر است جسم جوی و روح آب سایر است
english_translation: The word is like the nest and the meaning is the bird the body is the riverbed and the spirit is the rolling water

persian_text: همچنین هر جزو عالم میشمر اول و آخر در آرش در نظر
english_translation: Reckon every particle of the World like this bring its beginning and its end into consideration

persian_text: ده هزارش داد و خلعت درخورش خانهی شکر و ثنا گشت آن سرش
english_translation: He gave him the ten thousand and the robes of honour suitable to him his head became a house of thanksgiving and praise

persian_text: گفتمش این کفر مقضی نه قضاست هست آثار قضا این کفر راست
english_translation: I said to him This infidelity is the thing ordained it is not the ordainment this infidelity is truly the effects of the ordainment

persian_text: صید گیرد تیر هم با پر غیر لاجرم بیبهره است از لحم طیر
english_translation: Tis with alien feathers that the arrow captures its prey consequently it gets no share of the birds flesh

persian_text: مر سگی را لقمهی نانی ز در چون رسد بر در همیبندد کمر
english_translation: When to a dog there comes from the door a piece of bread he will gird up his loins at the door

persian_text: ز انکه آن تقلید صوفی از طمع عقل او بر بست از نور و لمع
english_translation: Because the Súfís imitation from desire debarred his understanding from the light and radiance

persian_text: هر که باشد شیر اسرار و امیر او بداند هر چه اندیشد ضمیر
english_translation: Any one that is the lion and prince of mysteries he will know all that the conscience thinks

persian_text: دعوت باز بطان را از آب به صحرا
english_translation: How the falcon invited the ducks to come from the water to the plain

persian_text: آب استاده که سیرستش نهان تازهتر خوشتر ز جوهای روان
english_translation: The still water whose course is hidden is fresher and sweeter than running brooks

persian_text: سر چارق را بیان کنای ایاز پیش چارق چیستت چندین نیاز
english_translation: O Ayáz declare the mystery of the rustic shoon and why in the presence of the shoon thou showest all this humility

persian_text: عاشق حق است او بهر نوال نیست جانش عاشق حسن و جمال
english_translation: He loves God for the sake of gain his soul is not in love with excellence and beauty

persian_text: بینهایت چون ندارد دو طرف کی بود او را میانه منصرف
english_translation: Inasmuch as the infinite has not two limits how should the mean be applicable to it

persian_text: پیش تو این حالت بد دولتست که دوادو اول و آخر لتست
english_translation: In thy sight this evil state is dawlat  whereof the beginning is dawádaw and the end lat

persian_text: من نمیکردم گزافه آن دعا همچو یوسف دیده بودم خوابها
english_translation: Not idly was I uttering that prayer like Joseph I had dreamed dreams

persian_text: خفته باشی بر لب جو خشکلب میدوی سوی سراب اندر طلب
english_translation: You are asleep with parched lips on the bank of the river and are running in search of water towards the mirage

persian_text: مرغ خانه بر زمین خوش میرود دانهچین و شاد و شاطر میدود
english_translation: the domestic fowl walks complacently on the earth it runs about picking grain and happy and bold

persian_text: گفت کودک سلم الله علیک یا رسولالله قد جنا الیک
english_translation: The child said God give peace unto thee O Messenger of Allah We have come to thee

persian_text: رو بدین بالا و پستیها بدو تا شوی تشنه و حرارت را گرو
english_translation: Go run on these hills and dales to the end that thou mayst become thirsty and a prey to heat

persian_text: تف برویش باز گردد بی شکی تف سوی گردون نیابد مسلکی
english_translation: Without any doubt his spittle will recoil upon his face spittle can find no path to heaven

persian_text: چشم حس همچون کف دستست و بس نیست کف را بر همهی او دسترس
english_translation: The eye of senseperception is only like the palm of the hand the palm hath not power to reach the whole of him

persian_text: حلقه در گوش مه زرگر شوی تا به ماه و تا ثریا بر شوی
english_translation: you will become a ring in the ear of the Moon that works in gold you will ascend to the moon and the Pleiades

persian_text: مرغ خاکی مرغ آبی همتنند لیک ضدانند آب و روغنند
english_translation: The landbird and the waterbird have the same body  but they are opposites they are water and oil

persian_text: گفت پیر آن دم مرید خویش را رو برای من بجو میای کیا
english_translation: Thereupon the Pír said to his disciple Go seek wine for me O noble sir

persian_text: بر سر بامی و قصری بس بلند حفظ حق را واقفیای هوشمند
english_translation: On the top of an exceedingly high terrace or pavilion Art thou conscious of Gods protection O intelligent man

persian_text: جنبش و آمد شد ما و اکتساب هست مفتاحی بر آن قفل و حجاب
english_translation: Our movement and our going to and fro and our acquisition is a key to that lock and barrier

persian_text: قصهی راز حلیمه گویمت تا زداید داستان او غمت
english_translation: I will tell you the story of Halímas mystic experience that her tale may clear away your trouble

persian_text: کردهایم آنها که از ما میسزید تا چه فرمایی توای شاه مجید
english_translation: We have done those deeds that were worthy of us consider what thou wilt command O glorious King

persian_text: پوستین یوسفان بکشافتند آنچ میکردند یکیک یافتند
english_translation: They tore the coats of the Josephs  and that which they gave they got piece by piece

persian_text: گفت اشتر چند داری چند گاو گفت نه این و نه آن ما را مکاو
english_translation: He asked How many camels have you How many oxen I have neither these nor those he replied do not dig at me

persian_text: برتن مرده بخواندم گشت حی بر سر لاشی بخواندم گشت شی
english_translation: I pronounced over the corpse it came to life I pronounced over nonentity it became entity

persian_text: خاک گور از مرد هم یابد شرف تا نهد بر گور او دل روی و کف
english_translation: The earth of the grave is ennobled by the man  so that the heart lays his face and hands on his grave

persian_text: از پی آنک دو درویش آمدند بر شه و بر قصر او موکب زدند
english_translation: Because two dervishes have come and marched in force against the King and his palace

persian_text: دوزخ اوصاف او عشقست و او سوخت مر اوصاف خود را مو بمو
english_translation: Love is the Hellfire of his attributes and it has burnt up the attributes of self hair by hair

persian_text: جز قرنفل یا سمن یا گل مچر رو به صحرای ختن با آن نفر
english_translation: Do not browse on aught but clove jasmine or roses go to the plain of Khutan in company with those personages

persian_text: احمقان سرور شدستند و ز بیم عاقلان سرها کشیده در گلیم
english_translation: The foolish have become leaders and from fear the wise have drawn their heads into the cloak

persian_text: خلم بهتر از چنین حلمای خدا که کند از نور ایمانم جدا
english_translation: Wrath is better O God than such forbearance  which separates me from the light of Faith

persian_text: بلک پیش از زادن تو سالها دیده باشندت ترا با حالها
english_translation: Nay they will have seen you years before your birth you together with all the circumstances

persian_text: تو همیگویی که میبینم ولیک دید آن را بس علامتهاست نیک
english_translation: Thou wilt say I am beholding it but there are many good signs of beholding it

persian_text: زانبهی برگ پیدا نیست شاخ برگ هم گمگشته از میوهی فراخ
english_translation: On account of the denseness of the leaves no boughs were visible the leaves too had become scant on account of the plenteous fruit

persian_text: حرصهای رفته اندر کتم غیب تاختن آورد سر بر زد ز جیب
english_translation: The greedy desires that had gone into the concealment of the Unseen rush out and display themselves

persian_text: تو مرا گویی که از بهر ثواب غسل ناکرده مرو در حوض آب
english_translation: You say to me For the sake of the reward do not go into the watertank without having washed

persian_text: که نه چربش دارد و نه نوش او سحر خواند میدمد در گوش او
english_translation: For she hath neither fat nor sweet she chants spells of magic and breathes into your ear

persian_text: رحم آمد مر شتر را گفت هین برجه و بر کودبان من نشین
english_translation: The camel took pity Hark said he jump up and sit on my hump

persian_text: کبر زشت و از گدایان زشتتر روز سرد و برف و آن گه جامهتر
english_translation: Pride is ugly and in beggars more ugly wet clothes after a cold snowy day

persian_text: باز پران کن حمام روح گیر در ره دعوت طریق نوح گیر
english_translation: Let the falcon fly and catch the spiritual dove in calling take the way of Noah

persian_text: گبر را در نیمشب یا صبحدم چون تقاضا آمد و درد شکم
english_translation: At midnight or dawn when the infidel felt an urgent need and stomachache

persian_text: نفی خورشید ازل بایست او کی بر آید این مراد او بگو
english_translation: What he wants is the extinction of the Sun of eternity Tell  how should this desire of his come to pass

persian_text: این بگفت و گریه در شد هایهای تا دل داود بیرون شد ز جای
english_translation: He said this and began to weep with loud cries of lamentation so that Davids heart was moved exceedingly

persian_text: دیدهی حسی زبون آفتاب دیدهی ربانیی جو و بیاب
english_translation: The sensuous eye is subject to the sun seek and find a divine eye

persian_text: مرد گلخنتاب از پری زر بسته بودی در میان زرین کمر
english_translation: On account of the plenty of gold a bathstoker might have tied a golden belt on his waist

persian_text: چون به درد آمد دل درویش از آن سر برون کردند هر سو در زمان
english_translation: When the heart of the dervish was pained by that  at once there put forth their heads on every side

persian_text: گفت بر جمله دلم سوزد به درد لیک ترسم امر را اهمال کرد
english_translation: He replied My heart burns with grief for them all but I am afraid to neglect the command

persian_text: مغز علم افزود کم شد پوستش زانک عاشق را بسوزد دوستش
english_translation: the kernel knowledge increases its rind decreases  because the lover is consumed by his beloved

persian_text: پیش او آمد هزاران مرد و زن کای دو عالم درج در یک پیرهن
english_translation: Thousands of men and women came to him  saying O thou in whose single shirt the two worlds are contained

persian_text: از برای غصهی نان سوختی دیدهی صبر و توکل دوختی
english_translation: Thou art consumed because of thy craving for bread thou hast closed the eye of selfdenial and trust in God

persian_text: جبر باشد پر و بال کاملان جبر هم زندان و بند کاهلان
english_translation: Necessitarianism is the wing and pinion of the perfect necessitarianism is also the prison and chains of the slothful

persian_text: شب به خواب اندر بگفتش هاتفی که خریدی آب حیوان و شفا
english_translation: At night whilst asleep a Voice said to him Thou hast bought the Water of Life and salvation

persian_text: آب را ببرید و جو را پاک کرد بعد از آن در جو روان کرد آب خورد
english_translation: Cut off the water and cleansed the riverbed then caused drinkingwater to flow in the riverbed

persian_text: آنچنان دلها که بدشان ما و من نعتشان شدت بل اشد قسوة
english_translation: Such hearts as had egoism were described nay harder

persian_text: چشم من چون دید روی آن قباد کثرت اعداد از چشمم فتاد
english_translation: When mine eye beheld the face of that emperor plurality vanished from my sight

persian_text: قسمت حقست مه را روی نغز دادهی بختست گل را بوی نغز
english_translation: The fair face of the moon is bestowed on it by God the sweet scent of the rose is the gift of Fortune

persian_text: ور تو میبینی که پایت بستهاند بر تو سرهنگان شه بنشستهاند
english_translation: And if you consider that your foot is shackled the kings officers are sitting over you

persian_text: بهر روز مرگ این دم مرده باش تا شوی با عشق سرمد خواجهتاش
english_translation: For the sake of your deathday be dead  now so that you may be with everlasting Love O fellowservant

persian_text: در فراخی عرصهی آن پاک جان تنگ آمد عرصهی هفتآسمان
english_translation: In comparison with the spaciousness of the range of that pure spirit  the expanse of the seven heavens became narrow

persian_text: کین دو دایه پوست را افزون کنند شحم و لحم و کبر و نخوت آکنند
english_translation: For these two nurses increase the skin they stuff it with fat and flesh and pride and arrogance

persian_text: بلعم باعور و ابلیس لعین ز امتحان آخرین گشته مهین
english_translation: Balam the son of Báúr and the accursed Iblís were disgraced at the ultimate test

persian_text: امر میآمد که نی طامع مشو چون ز پایت خار بیرون شد برو
english_translation: The command was coming Nay be not covetous inasmuch as the thorn is out of thy foot depart

persian_text: دیگران راگر ام و اب شد حجاب او ز ما یابید گوهرها به جیب
english_translation: if father and mother were an obstacle to others he derived the jewels in his pocket from Me

persian_text: او چو فرعونست در قحط آنچنان پیش موسی سر نهد لابهکنان
english_translation: It is like Pharaoh in famine it lays its head before Moses as he did making supplication

persian_text: جز نیاز و جز تضرع راه نیست زین تقلب هر قلم آگاه نیست
english_translation: There is no way but supplication and selfabasement not every pen is conscious of this subjection to control

persian_text: نیستم بیرحم بل زان هر سه پاک رحم بیشستم ز درد دردناک
english_translation: I am not pitiless nay I have greater pity than those three holy ones for the sorrow of the sorrowful

persian_text: جان تو بخرید آن دلدار خاص آن دم از گردن زدن کردت خلاص
english_translation: That elect beloved redeemed your life and saved you from beheading at that moment

persian_text: وام داران را ز عهده وا رهان همچو باران سبز کن فرش جهان
english_translation: Release those in debt from their responsibility like rain make the carpet of the world green

persian_text: قصر چیزی نیست ویران کن بدن گنج در ویرانیست ای میر من
english_translation: The palace is nothing ruin your body The treasure lies in the ruin O my prince

persian_text: گر نمودی عیب آن کار او ترا کس نبردی کش کشان آنسو ترا
english_translation: If He had shown unto thee the faultiness of that work no one dragging along  would have taken thee in that direction

persian_text: قبض دل قبض عوان شد لاجرم گشت محسوس آن معانی زد علم
english_translation: The contraction that grips the heart turns into the grip of the policeman inevitably those ideas become sensible and display themselves

persian_text: جوهر انسان بگیرد بر و بحر پیسه گاوان بسملان آن روز نحر
english_translation: The essence of Man will encompass land and sea the piebald cattle will be killed as victims on the Day of Slaughter

persian_text: که مرا رنجی است مضطر گشتهام من ز رنج از مخمصه بگذشتهام
english_translation: For I have a pain I am reduced to necessity because of the pain I have passed beyond starvation

persian_text: رفت از وی جنبش طبع و سکون از چه از انا الیه راجعون
english_translation: natural movement and rest have gone from itby what  By means of Verily unto Him we are returning

persian_text: جواب آن مثل کی منکران گفتند از رسالت خرگوش پیغام به پیل از ماه آسمان
english_translation: The answer to the parable which the unbelievers related concerning the hares being sent as ambassador with a message to the elephant from the Moon in heaven

persian_text: قصه پر جنگ و پر هستی و کین میفرستد پیش شاه نازنین
english_translation: He is sending to the gracious King a statement filled with wrangling and selfconceit and hatred

persian_text: چون سگ کهفی که از مردار رست بر سر خوان شهنشاهان نشست
english_translation: As the dog of the Cave which was freed from carrion and sat at the table of the emperors

persian_text: چشم تو روشن شود پایت دوان جسم تو جان گردد و جانت روان
english_translation: Your eye becomes bright your foot nimble your body becomes soul and your soul spirit

persian_text: گفت آن دینار اگر چه اندک است لیک موقوف غریو کودک است
english_translation: And said Though that dinar is little yet it is dependent on the boys outcry

persian_text: تا توانی در رضای قطب کوش تا قوی گردد کند صید وحوش
english_translation: So far as you can endeavour to satisfy the Qutb so that he may gain strength and hunt the wild beasts

persian_text: که نظرگاه خداوندست آن کز نظرانداز خورشیدست کان
english_translation: For that is the object of the Lords regard while the mine results from the suns casting looks

persian_text: کل آت آت آن را نقد دان دوست را در نزع و اندر فقد دان
english_translation: Everything that is coming will come deem it here and now deem thy friend to be in the deathagony and in the act of losing

persian_text: مر زبان را داد صد افسونگری وانک کان را داد زر جعفری
english_translation: gave a hundred enchantments to the tongue and that which gave the gold of Jafar to the mine

persian_text: عامل عشقست معزولش مکن جز به عشق خویش مشغولش مکن
english_translation: He is Loves agent do not deprive him of his employment do not let him be employed in aught but loving thee

persian_text: بیم سر با بیم سر با بیم دین امتحانی نیست ما را مثل این
english_translation: Fear for head with fear for heart with fear for religion there is no trial for us like this

persian_text: خاطر آرد بس شکال اینجا ولیک بسکلد اشکال را استور نیک
english_translation: Here the mind may bring many difficulties but a good beast will break the tether

persian_text: حلق بخشد خاک را لطف خدا تا خورد آب و بروید صد گیا
english_translation: The grace of God bestows a throat on the earth to the end that it may drink water and make a hundred herbs to grow

persian_text: بوی گل دیدی که آن جا گل نبود جوش مل دیدی که آن جا مل نبود
english_translation: Didst thou smell the scent of a rose where no rose was Didst thou see the foaming of wine where no wine was

persian_text: رو به تو کردند اکنون اهکنان ای که لطفت مجرمان را رهکنان
english_translation: Now they turn their faces towards thee uttering cries of lamentation O thou whose clemency is making way for sinners

persian_text: هین مگو فردا که فرداها گذشت تا به کلی نگذرد ایام کشت
english_translation: Beware Do not say Tomorrowfor tomorrows have passed Let not the days of sowing pass away altogether

persian_text: رب میگوید برو سوی سبب چون ز صنعم یاد کردیای عجب
english_translation: The Lord will say Betake thyself to the cause How hast thou remembered My work Oh wonderful

persian_text: رنجانیدن امیری خفتهای را که مار در دهانش رفته بود
english_translation: How an Amír harassed a sleeping man into whose mouth a snake had gone

persian_text: گفت اندر تو چه خاصیت بود گفت من رستم مکان ویران شود
