persian_text: گوش آنکس نوشد اسرار جلال کو چو سوسن صدزبان افتاد و لال
english_translation: The secrets of the Majesty are drunk in by the ear of that one who like the lily hath a hundred tongues and is dumb

persian_text: ناگهان تمثال گرگ هشتهای سر بر آورد از فراز پشتهای
english_translation: Suddenly the figure of a deserted wolf raised its head from the top of a hillock

persian_text: ای غم ارزاق ما بر خاطرت ای چو رزق عام احسان و برت
english_translation: O thou on whose heart the care for our means of livelihood  O thou whose beneficence and charity were the universal provision of sustenance

persian_text: نیمدانش دست او را بسته کرد جان ببرد الا که خود را خسته کرد
english_translation: Halfknowledge tied his hand he saved his life and only wounded himself

persian_text: جواب گفتن امیر مر آن شفیعان را
english_translation: How the Amír answered those neighbours of the ascetic

persian_text: از شتاب او و فحش اجتهاد غلغل و تشویش در ترمد فتاد
english_translation: Because of his haste and the enormity of his exertions tumult and commotion arose in Tirmid

persian_text: بس وصیت کرد و تخم وعظ کاشت چون زمینشان شوره بد سودی نداشت
english_translation: He gave many injunctions and sowed the seed of exhortation as their soil was nitrous  twas of no avail

persian_text: کو بلیگو جمله را سیلاب برد یا نهنگی خورد کل را کرد و مرد
english_translation: Where is anyone to say Yea The flood of has swept them all away or a leviathan has swallowed them all piecemeal

persian_text: دیدهی عقلت بدو بیرون جهد طعن اوت اندر کف طاعون نهد
english_translation: Through him the eye of thy reason starts out  his thrusts deliver thee into the hands of pestilence

persian_text: که بروای پیر این چه جست و جوست آن محمد را که عزل ما ازوست
english_translation: Saying Begone O old man What is this search after that Mohammed by whom we are deposed

persian_text: مال ناید با تو بیرون از قصور یار آید لیک آید تا به گور
english_translation: wealth will not come with thee out of thy palaces friend will come but he will come as far as thy grave

persian_text: شش جهت خالی ز صورت وین ندا شد پیاپی آن ندا را جان فدا
english_translation: the six directions were empty of form and this cry was continuousmay the soul be a ransom for that cry

persian_text: همچو نخلی برنیارد شاخها کرده موشانه زمین سوراخها
english_translation: He does not lift up branches like a datepalm he has bored holes in the earth after the fashion of mice

persian_text: صاحبخانه بخواهد مرد رفت روز فردا نک رسیدت لوت زفت
english_translation: The owner of the house will die depart  lo tomorrow a great deal of food will reach you

persian_text: این قدر عقلی که داری گم شود سر که عقل از وی بپرد دم شود
english_translation: This measure of understanding which you possess is lost a head from which the understanding flies away

persian_text: آنکسی را کش خدا حافظ بود مرغ و ماهی مر ورا حارس شود
english_translation: As for that one whom God protects birds and fish become his guardians

persian_text: گر بگویم شرح این بیحد شود مثنوی هشتاد تا کاغذ شود
english_translation: The gist is that whenever any one seeks the soul of the object sought by him is desiring him

persian_text: غیر حق جان نبی را یار نیست با قبول و رد خلقش کار نیست
english_translation: The prophets soul hath no friend except God he hath naught to do with the acceptance or rejection of the people

persian_text: باز فرمود او که اندر هر قضا مر مسلمان را رضا باید رضا
english_translation: Again he said that the Moslem must be satisfied in every ordainment must be satisfied

persian_text: حال من اکنون برون از گفتن است این چه میگویم نه احوال من است
english_translation: Now my state is beyond telling this which I am telling is not my state

persian_text: کز ضرورت هست مرداری حلال که تحری نیست در کعبهی وصال
english_translation: For in case of necessity a carcase is lawful  but there is no need to investigate in the Kaba of union

persian_text: گفت ما دو بندگان کوی تو کردمش آزاد من بر روی تو
english_translation: He replied We are two slaves in thy street I set him free for thy sake

persian_text: تا به بغداد آمد آن هنگامهجو تا نهد هنگامهای بر چارسو
english_translation: Till the wouldbe showman arrived at Baghdád that he might set up a public show at the crossroads

persian_text: نام حق بستاند از تو داد منمن به نام حق سپردم جان و تن
english_translation: The Name of God will take vengeance from thee on my behalf I commit my soul and body to the Name of God

persian_text: عجزها داری تو در پیشای لجوج وقت شد پنهانیان را نک خروج
english_translation: O perverse man thou hast many a helpless plight before thee the hour comes lo the hideaways will emerge

persian_text: صورت خندان نقش از بهر تست تا از آن صورت شود معنی درست
english_translation: The pictures smiling appearance is for your sake in order that by means of that picture the reality may be established

persian_text: رو بایشان کرد و گفت ای مشفقان از کجا آمد بگویید این عوان
english_translation: He turned his face to them and said O kindly men tell  whence came this myrmidon

persian_text: بر درید آن گوسفندان را بخشم که ز چوپان خرد بستند چشم
english_translation: In wrath he tore to pieces those sheep which shut their eyes to the shepherd Wisdom

persian_text: کو همیبیند شما را از کمین که شما او را نمیبینید هین
english_translation: For he sees you from ambush so that ye see him not Take care

persian_text: امة الکفران اضل اعمالهم امة الایمان اصلح بالهم
english_translation: He causeth the works of the unbelieving people to be lost He maketh the state of the believing people to prosper

persian_text: چونک عاشق توبه کرد اکنون بترس کو چو عیاران کند بر دار درس
english_translation: Since the lover has repented now beware  for he will lecture like the adepts  on the gallows

persian_text: چونک هنگام فراق جان شود دیو دلال در ایمان شود
english_translation: When the hour arrives for the spirits parting  the Devil becomes a broker of the pearl of Faith

persian_text: جان گشاید سوی بالا بالها در زده تن در زمین چنگالها
english_translation: The spirit unfolds its wings upwards the body has stuck its claws in the earth

persian_text: هر چه گویم من به جان نیکوی قوم نقص گفتم گشته ناقصگوی قوم
english_translation: Whatsoever I say with my soul in praise of the company  I have depreciated  I have become a disparager of the company

persian_text: بردریدی در سخن پردهی قیاسگر نبودی سمع سامع را نعاس
english_translation: If there had not been somnolence in the hearers hearing the veil of logical reasoning would have been torn asunder in the discourse

persian_text: پس به معنی باغ باشد این نه آب پس مشو عریان چو بلقیس از حباب
english_translation: Therefore this is really an orchard not water do not then like Bilqís strip yourself from the waves

persian_text: گر حلال آمد پی قوت عوام طالبان دوست را آمد حرام
english_translation: If it is lawful as sustenance for the common folk it is unlawful to those who seek the Beloved

persian_text: چند در دهلیز قاضیای گواه حبس باشی ده شهادت از بگاه
english_translation: How long O witness wilt thou remain under detention in the court of the Judge Give thy testimony betimes

persian_text: گفت عبدالمطلب کین دم کجاستای علیم السر نشان ده راه راست
english_translation: Abdu lMuttalib said Where is he now O Thou that knowest the secret  point out the right way

persian_text: پیششان بردم بسی جام رحیق سنگ شد آبش به پیش این فریق
english_translation: I bore to them many a cup of pure wine its juice turned to stone before this company

persian_text: وانک ناموسیست خود از زیر زیر غمزه دزدد میدهد مالش به کیر
english_translation: And even he that has regard for decorum steals covert glances et penem fricat And even he that has regard for decorum steals covert glances rubbing penis

persian_text: آفتابی رفت در کازهی هلال در تقاضا که ارحنا یا بلال
english_translation: A Sun went into the hut of the newmoon making urgent demands and saying Refresh us O Bilál

persian_text: عشق صورت در دل شهزادگان چون خلش میکرد مانند سنان
english_translation: Since love of the pictured form was stabbing the hearts of the princes like a spearpoint

persian_text: یا بود کز عکس آن جوهای خمر مست گردم بو برم از ذوق امر
english_translation: Or perchance by the reflexion of those rivers of wine I may be intoxicated and obtain a scent of the delight of the command

persian_text: هست بیرنگی اصول رنگها صلحها باشد اصول جنگها
english_translation: Colourlessness is the origin of colours peaces are the origins of wars

persian_text: ده چراغ ار حاضر آید در مکان هر یکی باشد به صورت غیر آن
english_translation: If ten lamps are present in place each differs in form from another

persian_text: پس چو تن باشد جهاد و اعتقاد جان این کشتن نباتست و حصاد
english_translation: Therefore the hard struggle and his firm religious conviction are like the body the soul of this sowing is the growth and its harvesting

persian_text: پاک سبحانی که سیبستان کند در غمام حرفشان پنهان کنند
english_translation: The Holy Transcendent One who makes the appleorchard conceals them in a mist of words

persian_text: حالها موقوف عزم و رای او زنده از نفخ مسیحآسای او
english_translation: States are dependent on his decision and judgement vivified by his Messiahlike breath

persian_text: چون ید الله فوق أیدیهم بود دست ما را دست خود فرمود احد
english_translation: Since there is the hand of God is above their hands the One has declared our hand to be His hand

persian_text: در کدامین دفترست این شرع نو گاو را تو باز ده یا حبس رو
english_translation: In what book is this new statute  Give back the cow or go to prison

persian_text: پر ز سر تا پای زشتی و گناه تسخر و خنبک زدن بر اهل راه
english_translation: Filled from top to bottom with foulness and sin with mockery and jeering at the followers of the Way

persian_text: پند فعلی خلق را جذابتر که رسد در جان هر باگوش و کر
english_translation: The exhortation of act draws people more powerfully for it reaches the soul of every one that hath hearing and the deaf

persian_text: چون سلیمان کرد آغاز بنا پاک چون کعبه همایون چون منی
english_translation: When Solomon began the buildingholy like the Kaba august like Miná

persian_text: هم تو شاه و هم تو لشکر هم تو تخت هم تو نیکوبخت باشی هم تو بخت
english_translation: Thou wilt be sovereign army and throne all together thou wilt be both the fortunate and Fortune

persian_text: گرچه بشکستند حامت قوم مست آنک مست از تو بود عذریش هست
english_translation: Although the drunken fellows broke Thy cup there is an excuse for him that is intoxicated by Thee

persian_text: کالبد نامهست اندر وی نگر هست لایق شاه را آنگه ببر
english_translation: The body is a letter look into it whether it is worthy of the King then take it

persian_text: جان او مجنون تنش شمشیر او واستان شمشیر را زان زشتخو
english_translation: His spirit is the madman and his body is his sword take away the sword from that wicked man

persian_text: کرد یک داماد صالح اختیار که بد او فخر همه خیل و تبار
english_translation: He chose a pious soninlaw who was the pride of the whole clan and stock

persian_text: کان رسول حق بگفت اندر بیان اینک منهومان هما لا یشبعان
english_translation: For the Messenger of God said in explanation this There are two greedy ones who are never satisfied

persian_text: گفت با صدر جهان چون بستدم ای ببسته بر من ابواب کرم
english_translation: He said to the Sadri Jahán  how I have received  O thou who didst shut the doors of generosity against me

persian_text: بخت ما را بر درید آن بخت او تخت ما شد سرنگون از تخت او
english_translation: His fortune tore up our fortune our throne was overturned by his throne

persian_text: عارفان ز آغاز گشته هوشمند از غم و احوال آخر فارغاند
english_translation: the gnostics having become conscious of the beginning are free from anxiety and the ultimate conditions

persian_text: گور چه از صید غیر دوست دور جمله شیر و شیرگیر و مست نور
english_translation: What onager  They are far from hunting aught but the Beloved They all are lions and lioncatchers and intoxicated with the Light

persian_text: تو بدان غره مشو کش ساختی در دل خلقان هراس انداختی
english_translation: Be not deluded by that which thou hast contrived thou hast cast terror into the hearts of the people

persian_text: بود شاهی بود او را بندهای مرده عقلی بود و شهوتزندهای
english_translation: There was a king he had a slave he was one whose reason was dead and whose lust was alive

persian_text: آنکه غافل بود از کشت بهار او چه داند قیمت این روزگار
english_translation: He that was heedless of the sowing and the springtide how should he know the value of this lifetime

persian_text: گر یکی دم تو به غفلت واهلیش او رود فرسنگها سوی حشیش
english_translation: If you carelessly leave him free for one moment he will go leagues in the direction of the herbage

persian_text: وصف جبریلی دریشان بود رفت تنگ بود آن خانه و آن وصف زفت
english_translation: The qualities of Gabriel were in them and departed that house was narrow and those qualities grand

persian_text: در بشر رو پوش کرده ست آفتاب فهم کن و الله اعلم بالصواب
english_translation: The Sun has veiled Himself in Man apprehend  and God knows best what is right

persian_text: دل ببیند سر بدان چشم صفی آن حشایش که شد از عامه خفی
english_translation: The heart with that pure eye sees occultly the herbs that are invisible to the vulgar

persian_text: آمدیم اکنون به طاوس دورنگ کو کند جلوه برای نام و ننگ
english_translation: Now we come to the twocoloured peacock who displays himself for the sake of name and fame

persian_text: آن تقاضا کرد آن نان و نمک که ز شستت وا رهانم ای سمک
english_translation: that bread and salt demanded that I should deliver thee O fish from the net

persian_text: هم مخبط دینشان و حکمشان از پی طومارهای کژ بیان
english_translation: Moreover their religion and their law became corrupted in consequence of the scrolls which set forth all perversely

persian_text: چون شکستهدل شدی از حال خویش جابر اشکستگان دیدی به پیش
english_translation: Since it would have become brokenhearted on account of its state it would have seen before it Him who mends them that are broken

persian_text: میدود بر امر تو فرزند نو که منم جزوت که کردیاش گرو
english_translation: Your young son runs at your command saying I am the part of thee which thou didst deposit

persian_text: گفت ای شه مژده حاجاتت رواستگر غریبی آیدت فردا ز ماست
english_translation: And said Good tidings O king Thy prayers are granted If tomorrow a stranger come for thee he is from me

persian_text: در رحم زادن جنین را رفتن است در جهان او را ز نو بشکفتن است
english_translation: To the embryo in the womb birth is a going  in the world it blossoms anew

persian_text: آب شوری نیست در مان عطش وقت خوردنگر نماید سرد و خوش
english_translation: A briny water is no remedy for thirst if it seem cold and delicious at the moment of drinking

persian_text: هر چه یابد او بسوزد بر درد چون نیابد هیچ خود را میخورد
english_translation: Whatsoever he finds he destroys and tears to pieces when he finds nothing he devours himself

persian_text: صوفیان را پیش رو موضع دهند کاینهی جاناند و ز آیینه بهند
english_translation: They give the Súfís the place in front of their countenance for they are a mirror for the soul and better than a mirror

persian_text: عالم وهم و خیال طمع و بیم هست رهرو را یکی سدی عظیم
english_translation: The world of imagination and the phantom of hope and fear is a great obstacle to the traveller

persian_text: گر سخن خواهی که گویی چون شکر صبر کن از حرص و این حلوا مخور
english_translation: If you wish to utter words like sugar refrain from concupiscence and do not eat this sweetmeat

persian_text: که ترازوی حق است و کیلهاش مخلص است از مکر دیو و حیلهاش
english_translation: Saying  He is Gods scales and measure he is the means of deliverance from the deceit and cunning of the Devil

persian_text: کامتحان کردیم و ما را کی رسد امتحان تو اگر نبود حسد
english_translation: Saying We have put to the test and how should testing thee occur to us unless there be envy

persian_text: گفت حق آن طفل را از فضل خویش موج را گفتم فکن در بیشهایش
english_translation: God said Of My grace I bade the waves cast that child into a forest

persian_text: گفت سازندهی سبب را آن نفسای خدا تو رحمتی کن بر عسس
english_translation: At that moment he said to the Maker of the means  O God have mercy on the nightpatrol

persian_text: هر که کردی ناگهان با لب سال زو نبردی زین گنه یک حبه مال
english_translation: And any one who suddenly begged with his lips was punished for this offence by not getting from him a mite of money

persian_text: داد ده ما را ازین غم کن جدا دستگیرای دست تو دست خدا
english_translation: Do justice relieve us from this sorrow take our hand  O thou whose hand is the hand of God

persian_text: دل مگر رنجور باشد بد دهان که نداند چاشنی این و آن
english_translation: Sick surely and illsavoured is the heart that knows not the taste of this and that

persian_text: گر بپوشد بهر مکر آن جامه را عاریه است او و فریبد عامه را
english_translation: If he puts on that garment for the purpose of deceiving it is borrowed in order that he may beguile the common folk

persian_text: دل فراخان را بود دست فراخ چشم کوران را عثار سنگلاخ
english_translation: They whose hearts are wide have wide hands they whose eyes are blind have to stumble on stony ground

persian_text: نوحهها کرد او بر جان خویش روی عزرائیل دیده پیش پیش
english_translation: He was uttering cries of mourning over his soul he saw the face of Azrael nearer and nearer

persian_text: چون سر و ماهیت جان مخبرست هر که او آگاهتر با جانترست
english_translation: Since consciousness is the inmost nature and essence of the soul the more aware one is the more spiritual is he

persian_text: پس سلیمان گفت ای هدهد رواست کز تو در اول قدح این درد خاست
english_translation: Then Solomon said O hoopoe is it right that these dregs have risen from thee at the first cup

persian_text: جامه شویی کرد خواهی ای فلان رو مگردان از محلهی گازران
english_translation: Wouldst thou wash thy garment  O soandso do not avert thy face from the bleachers quarter

persian_text: قطرهای کاو در هوا شد یا که ریخت از خزینهی قدرت تو کی گریخت
english_translation: The drop that vanished in the air or was spilled when did it flee from the storehouse of Thy omnipotence

persian_text: زیرکان که مویها بشکافتند علم هیات را به جان دریافتند
english_translation: The acute men who have split hairs and with their soul have apprehended the science of astronomy

persian_text: بعد نه سال آمد او هم عاریه گشت پیدا باز شد متواریه
english_translation: After nine years he came temporarily he appeared and disappeared again

persian_text: تفسیر این آیت کی ان تستفتحوا فقد جائکم الفتح
english_translation: Commentary on the verse  If ye ask for a decision the decision has indeed come to you

persian_text: کز سفرها ماه کیخسرو شود بی سفرها ماه کی خسرو شود
english_translation: For by traveling the moon becomes Kay Khusraw how should it become an emperor without traveling

persian_text: لاف شیخی در جهان انداخته خویشتن را بایزیدی ساخته
english_translation: boast far and wide of being Shaykhs and make out that they have the rank of Báyazíd

persian_text: خیز شیران خدا بین گورگیر تو چو سگ چونی بزرقی کورگیر
english_translation: Arise behold the lions of God who catch the onager how art thou like a dog catching the blind with a hypocrisy

persian_text: یا گرو بسته ست با یاران بدین که بترساند مرا زین همنشین
english_translation: Or he has wagered with his friends that he will make me afraid of this companion

persian_text: لیک چون آمیخت با خاک کرب کی دهند این زیر و آن بم آن طرب
english_translation: But since it is mingled with the earth of sorrow how should this treble and bass give the same delight

persian_text: پشت خر دکان و مال و مکسب است در قلبت مایهی صد قالب است
english_translation: the back of the ass is shop and wealth and gain the pearl of your heart is the stock for a hundred bodies

persian_text: مدتی حس را بشو ز آب عیان اینچنین دان جامهشوی صوفیان
english_translation: Wash thy senses for a while with the water of clairvoyance know that the garmentwashing of the Súfís is like this

persian_text: آن چه حق آموخت کرم پیله را هیچ پیلی داند آن گون حیله را
english_translation: That which God taught to the silkwormdoes any elephant know such a device

persian_text: تو بدان عادت که او را پیش از این خورده بودی ای وجود نازنین
english_translation: According as thou hadst formerly been in the habit of eating it O gracious being

persian_text: لیک معنیشان بود در سه مقام در مراتب هم ممیز هم مدام
english_translation: But their reality in the three categories is both differentiated in grades and permanent

persian_text: چه عجب که مرغ گردد مست او هم شنود آهن ندای دست او
english_translation: What wonder that a bird should be enraptured by him since iron obeyed the call of his hand

persian_text: هر که باشد همنشین دوستان هست در گلخن میان بوستان
english_translation: Whoever is sitting with friends is amidst a flowergarden in a bathfurnace

persian_text: پشت با وی کن تو رو در قبله آر وانگهان از قوس تیری بر گذار
english_translation: Turn your back to it and face the qibla and then let loose an arrow from your bow

persian_text: گویدت تریاق از من جو سپر که ز زهرم من به تو نزدیکتر
english_translation: The antidote says to you Seek from me a shield for I am nearer than the poison to thee

persian_text: تو ز رعنایان مجو هین کارزار تو ز طاوسان مجو صید و شکار
english_translation: Beware Do not thou request sybarites to fight do not request peacocks to engage in the hunt and the chase

persian_text: تا بداند که به زر طامع نهایم ما زر از زرآفرین آوردهایم
english_translation: That she may know we do not covet gold we have gotten gold from the goldCreator

persian_text: همچو شاخی که ببری از درخت سر کند قوت ز شاخ نیکبخت
english_translation: As when you lop off a branch from a tree vigour is imparted to the wellconditioned branches

persian_text: چون ز چشم خویش و خلقان دور شد همچو عنقا در جهان مشهور شد
english_translation: When he became far from his own and the peoples eyes he became renowned in the world like the Anqá

persian_text: گر نیابی نبودت هرگز ملال ور بیابی آن به تو کردم حلال
english_translation: If you cannot find it you will never weary  and if you find it I grant you the right of possession

persian_text: زین دو جوشش قوت حسها شدم روح گشتم پس ترا استا شدم
english_translation: By reason of these two boilings I became strength to the senses I became spirit then I became thy teacher

persian_text: یک سر مو از تو نتواند برید چون قلم بر تو چنان خطی کشید
english_translation: None could cut from thee the tip of a single hair since the Pen has written against thee such a line

persian_text: ناریان مر ناریان را جاذباند نوریان مر نوریان را طالباند
english_translation: Those of the Fire attract those of the Fire those of the Light seek those of the Light

persian_text: تا که کر و فر و زر بخشی او همچو عنبر بو دهد در گفت و گو
english_translation: So that his pomp and magnificence and lavishing of gold may yield a perfume like ambergris in song

persian_text: فعل باران بهاری با درخت آید از انفاسشان در نیکبخت
english_translation: From their breaths there comes in him who is fortunate the effect of the spring rain on the tree

persian_text: آن چه شیرین است او شد ناردانگ و آنکه پوسیده ست نبود غیر بانگ
english_translation: That which is sweet becomes pomegranatesyrup and that which is rotten is naught but noise

persian_text: همچو عیسی بر سرش گیرد فرات که ایمنی از غرقه در آب حیات
english_translation: whom the vast river takes on its head  like Jesus saying In the Water of Life thou art safe from drowning

persian_text: گر نخواهی هر دمی این خفتخیز کن ز خاک پایی مردی چشم تیز
english_translation: Unless thou desire this incessant lying down and rising up  sharpen thine eye with the dust on the foot of a holy man

persian_text: گر بدانی رحم این محمود راد خوش بگویی عاقبت محمود باد
english_translation: If you come to know the mercifulness of this noble Mahmúd you will cry joyously May the end be praised

persian_text: عاقبت زینها بخواهی ماندن هین کرا خواهی در آن دم خواندن
english_translation: At the last you will be left without these things hark unto whom will you call then

persian_text: چون به یک شب مه برید ابراج را از چه منکر میشوی معراج را
english_translation: Since the moon traversed the signs of the zodiac in one night wherefore wilt thou disbelieve the Ascension

persian_text: جنبش ما هر دمی خود اشهدست که گواه ذوالجلال سرمدست
english_translation: our movement is really a continual profession of faith which bears witness to the Eternal Almighty One

persian_text: ما که باطنبین جملهی کشوریم دل ببینیم و به ظاهر ننگریم
english_translation: We who see the inward of the whole world see the heart and look not on the outward form

persian_text: طفل تو گرچه که کودکخو بدست هر دو عالم خود طفیل او بدست
english_translation: Though thy infant is childish verily both the worlds are his parasites

persian_text: جز توکل جز که تسلیم تمام در غم و راحت همه مکر است و دام
english_translation: Excepting trust and complete resignation in sorrow and joy all is a deceit and snare

persian_text: بانگ زد آن دزد کای میر دیار اولین بارست جرمم زینهار
english_translation: The thief cried out saying O Prince of the land this is my first offence Mercy

persian_text: از هوارانی دماغت فاسدست مشک و عنبر پیش مغزت کاسدست
english_translation: Thy brain is corrupted by sensual indulgence to thy sense musk and ambergris are unsalable

persian_text: گر ازین زودتر مرا معبر بدی این حجاب و پردهام کمتر بدی
english_translation: If my passing had taken place sooner this screen and veil of mine would have been less

persian_text: بر هوا انداخت هر یک را گزاف تا همیزد بر زمین میشد شکاف
english_translation: She tossed each one in the air recklessly so that he dashed on the earth and was cloven asunder

persian_text: پس رسول آن گفتشان را فهم کرد گفت آن خنده نبودم از نبرد
english_translation: The Prophet then apprehended their words and said That laughter of mine was not from hostility

persian_text: تا چون زن عشوه خری ای بیخرد از دروغ و عشوه کی یابی مدد
english_translation: So long as thou wouldst fain be wheedled like a woman O man without wisdom  how wilt thou be helped by lies and wheedling

persian_text: ظاهرست و هرکسی پی میبرد کو بیان که گم شود در وی خرد
english_translation: It is plain and every one finds the way  where is the exposition in which the intellect becomes lost

persian_text: چون به یک دکان بگفتی عمرم این عمر را نان فروشید از کرم
english_translation: When you say at one shop I am Umar kindly sell bread to this Umar

persian_text: زیر آن باطن یکی بطن سوم که درو گردد خردها جمله گم
english_translation: And beneath that inward a third interior  wherein all intellects become lost

persian_text: خصم در ده رفت و حارس نیز نیست بهر خلوت سخت نیکو مسکنیست
english_translation: The enemy has gone into the country and the caretaker is not there either it is a very good place for meeting in private

persian_text: مثنوی ما دکان وحدتست غیر واحد هرچه بینی آن بتست
english_translation: Our Mathnawi is the shop for Unity anything that you see except the One God is an idol

persian_text: خوی آن هاروت و ماروتای پسر چون بگشت و دادشان خوی بشر
english_translation: O son when the nature of Hárút and Márút was changed and He bestowed on them the nature of man

persian_text: هر کجا بیماری مزمن بدی یاد اوشان داروی شافی شدی
english_translation: Wherever there was a chronic sickness mention of him was always their healing medicine

persian_text: با سببها از مسبب غافلی سوی این روپوشها زان مایلی
english_translation: with causes you are forgetful of the Causer hence you are inclining towards these veils

persian_text: هر دمی از وی همیآید أ لست جوهر و اعراض میگردند هست
english_translation: Every moment there is coming from Him  Am not I  and substance and accidents are becoming existent

persian_text: گفت چون شاه کرم میدان رود عین هر بیآلتی آلت شود
english_translation: The wife said When the gracious King goes into the field  the essence of every lack of means becomes a means

persian_text: مثنوی از تو هزاران شکر داشت در دعا و شکر کفها بر فراشت
english_translation: On thy account the Mathnawí had thousands of thanksgivings  it lifted up its hands in prayer and thanksgiving

persian_text: کیف یاتی النظم لی والقافیه بعد ما ضاعت اصول العافیه
english_translation: How should poesy and rhyme come to me after the foundations of sanity are destroyed

persian_text: پس نظرگاه شعاع آن آهن است پس نظرگاه خدا دل نی تن است
english_translation: Therefore the theatre of the rays is that iron therefore the theatre of God is the heart not the body

persian_text: خشم بر شاهان شه و ما را غلام خشم را هم بستهام زیر لگام
english_translation: Anger is king over kings and to me it is a slave even anger I have bound under the bridle

persian_text: این زمین و سختیان پردهست و بس اصل روزی از خدا دان هر نفس
english_translation: This soil and morocco are only a veil  know that at every moment the source of livelihood is in God

persian_text: دو دهان داریم گویا همچو نی یک دهان پنهانست در لبهای وی
english_translation: We have two vocal mouths like the reed one mouth is hidden in his lips

persian_text: چون جفا آری فرستد گوشمال تا ز نقصان وا روی سوی کمال
english_translation: When you commit wrong He sends chastisement to the end that you may go back from imperfection towards perfection

persian_text: ور شود صد تو که باشد این زبان که بجنباند به کف پردهی عیان
english_translation: if it be hundredfold who is this tongue that it should move with its hand the veil of clairvoyance

persian_text: کار هر نازکدلی نبود قتال که گریزد از خیالی چون خیال
english_translation: Fighting is not the business of any faintheart who runs away from a spectre  like a spectre

persian_text: چون شکاری مینمایدشان ز دور جمله حمله میفزایند آن طیور
english_translation: When an object of chase appears to them from afar all those birds increase their onset

persian_text: بر جهد و ان خار محکمتر زند عاقلی باید که خاری بر کند
english_translation: He jumps and the thorn strikes more firmly  it needs an intelligent person to extract a thorn

persian_text: حازمی باید که ره تا ده برد حزم نبود طمع طاعون آورد
english_translation: A prudent man is needed to find the way to the village prudence be absent cupidity brings calamity

persian_text: آن شتربان سیه را با شتر سوی من آرید با فرمان مر
english_translation: Bring to me that negro cameldriver along with the camel by force if need be

persian_text: بنگرم از اوج با چشم یقین من ببینم آب در قعر زمین
english_translation: I gaze from the zenith with the eye of certainty and I see the water at the bottom of the earth

persian_text: پا برهنه چون رود در خارزار جز بوقفه و فکرت و پرهیزگار
english_translation: How should a barefooted man go into the thornthicket save with halting and reflection and cautiously

persian_text: چونک بر گردد ازو آن ساجدش داند او کان زهر بود و موبدش
english_translation: When his adorer turns away from him he knows that that was poisonous and destructive to him

persian_text: گرگ نادیده که منع او بود دزد نادیده که دفع او شود
english_translation: He has not seen the wolf so as to prevent him he has not seen the robber so as to repel him

persian_text: آن ضمیر رو ترش را پاسدار آن ترش را چون شکر شیرین شمار
english_translation: Pay watchful regard to the sourlooking thought deem that sour one to be sweet as sugar

persian_text: چون یقین دیدم عنایتهای تو بر وی او دریست از دریای تو
english_translation: Since I saw with certainty Thy favours towards him he is a pearl of Thy sea

persian_text: گفت یا رب میفریبد او مرا میفریبد او فریبندهی ترا
english_translation: He said O Lord he is deceiving me he is deceiving Thy deceiver

persian_text: پس قضا را خواجه از مقضی بدان تا شکالت دفع گردد در زمان
english_translation: Therefore know  sire the ordainment from the thing ordained so that thy difficulty may be removed at once

persian_text: وز جهان چون رحم بیرون روی از زمین در عرصهی واسع شوی
english_translation: And mayst go forth from this womblike world and mayst go from the earth into a wide expanse

persian_text: تو چو جانی ما مثال دست و پا قبض و بسط دست از جان شد روا
english_translation: Thou art as the spirit we are like hand and foot the closing and opening of the hand is due to the spirit

persian_text: خویش را تسلیم کن بر دام مزد و انگه از خود بیز خود چیزی بدزد
english_translation: Yield yourself up to the snare of the reward and then beside yourself steal something from yourself

persian_text: گرچه نشنید آن موکل آن سخن رفت در گوشی که آن بد من لدن
english_translation: Though the custodian did not hear those words they entered into the ear that was from the presence

persian_text: گفت مومن امشب این خورده شود صبر را بنهیم تا فردا بود
english_translation: The true believer said Let this be eaten tonight let us put away selfdenial till tomorrow

persian_text: چون ملالم گیرد از سفلی صفات بر پرم همچون طیور الصافات
english_translation: When disgust at the qualities of the low seizes me I soar up like the birds which spread their pinions

persian_text: تا گره با نی بود هم راز نیست همنشین آن لب و آواز نیست
english_translation: Whilst the reed is knotted it is not a sharer of secrets it is not the companion of the lip and voice

persian_text: اشتری را دید روزی استری چونک با او جمع شد در آخری
english_translation: One day a mule saw a camel since he had been put into a stable with him

persian_text: کو یکی مرغی ضعیفی بیگناه و اندرون او سلیمان با سپاه
english_translation: Where is a bird weak and innocent and within him Solomon with his host

persian_text: گر نباشد گندم محبوب نوش چه برد گندمنمای جو فروش
english_translation: If there be no savoury wheat what shall he get who sells barley pretending that it is wheat

persian_text: نوحه خواهد کرد بر محرومیم چشم خواهد بست از مظلومیم
english_translation: It will mourn for my deprivation  it will shut its eyes to my abjectness

persian_text: هم ز لطف و عکس آب با شرف پرده شد بر روی آب اجزای کف
english_translation: Tis from the purity and translucence of the noble Water that the particles of foam have veiled the face of the Water

persian_text: حفظ کردم من نکردم ردتان در وجود جد جد جدتان
english_translation: I protected you I did not spurn you in the bodies of the ancestors of the ancestors of your ancestors

persian_text: دید مریم صورتی بس جانفزا جانفزایی دلربایی در خلا
english_translation: Mary in her chamber saw a form that gave increase of life a lifeincreasing heartravishing one

persian_text: کان فلکپیمای میمونبال چست این زمان در عشق و اندر دام تست
english_translation: Saying That heavensurveying nimble of blessed wing is at this time in love and in thy net

persian_text: شیر گفت آری و لیکن هم ببین جهدهای انبیا و مومنین
english_translation: Yes said the lion but at the same time consider the exertions of the prophets and the true believers

persian_text: چوب را ماری کنم من هفت سر که نزاید ماده مار او را ز نر
english_translation: I make the wooden staff a sevenheaded dragon which the female dragon does not bring to birth from the male

persian_text: ایستاده پیش سلطان ظاهرش در ریاض غیب جان طایرش
english_translation: His exterior stood before the Sultan his soaring spirit was in the meadows of the Unseen

persian_text: خشم شحنه شعلهی نار از هواست چارمیخ و هیبت دار از هواست
english_translation: The anger of the police magistrate is a fiery spark from selfwill crucifixion and the awfulness of the gallows are of selfwill

persian_text: حیرت اندر حیرت آمد این قصص بیهشی خاصگان اندر اخص
english_translation: This tale of the elect losing their senses in the most elect is amazement on amazement

persian_text: زین غمام بانگ و حرف و گفت و گوی پردهای کز سیب ناید غیر بوی
english_translation: From this mist of sound and words and talk a screen that naught of the apple comes save the scent

persian_text: بقیهی قصهی متعرضان پیلبچگان
english_translation: The remainder of the Story of those who molested the young elephants

persian_text: باز گوای باز عرش خوش شکار تا چه دیدی این زمان از کردگار
english_translation: Tell it forth O falcon of the empyrean that findest goodly prey that what thou hast seen at this time from the Maker

persian_text: گفت میدانم که نازی میکنی یا ز ناموس احترازی میکنی
english_translation: He replied I know that you are showing disdain or holding aloof in regard for your reputation

persian_text: دایهاش پنبهست اول لیک اخیر میرساند شعلهها او تا اثیر
english_translation: Cotton is its nurse at first but in the end it carries its flames up to the aether

persian_text: آنک بهر خود زند او ضامنست وآنک بهر حق زند او آمنست
english_translation: He who strikes for his own sake is responsible while he who strikes for Gods sake is secure

persian_text: یک جزیرهی سبز هست اندر جهان اندرو گاویست تنها خوشدهان
english_translation: There is in the world a green island where a sweetmouthed cow lives alone

persian_text: ور خبر آید که شه رحمت نمود از مسلمانان فکند آن را به جود
english_translation: And if news come that the king has shown mercy and has generously taken off that from the Moslems

persian_text: از حضور نور بخش مصطفا پیش خاطر آمد او را آن دعا
english_translation: Through the lightgiving presence of Mustafá  that prayer came into his mind

persian_text: مصطفی ساکن شدی ز انداختن باز هجران آوریدی تاختن
english_translation: Mustafá would desist from casting  separation again made an attack

persian_text: روان شدن خواجه به سوی ده
english_translation: The Khwajas departure to the country

persian_text: خود ز بیم این دم بیمنتها باز خوان فأبین أن یحملنها
english_translation: Truly from fear of this infinite breath  recite but they refused to bear it

persian_text: مادران را داب من آموختم چون بود لطفی که من افروختم
english_translation: I have taught mothers to care  how must be the kindness that I have kindled

persian_text: دی شوی بینی تو اخراج بهار لیل گردی بینی ایلاج نهار
english_translation: thou become December  thou wilt experience the bringing forth of Spring thou become night thou wilt experience the advent of day

persian_text: من چو صبح صادقم از نور رب که نگردد گرد روزم هیچ شب
english_translation: I am of the light of the Lord like the true dawn for no night prowls around my day

persian_text: ای که تو هم عاشقی بر عقل خویش خویش بر صورت پرستان دیده بیش
english_translation: You who are in love with your intellect deeming yourself superior to worshippers of form

persian_text: کاشکی من گرد گلخن گشتمی بر در این خانقه نگذشتمی
english_translation: Would that I had loitered round the bathstove and had not passed by the door of this monastery

persian_text: جام صحت را نبودی سنگ تب آمنی با خوف ناوردی کرب
english_translation: If there were no stone of fever to shatter the cup of health and if fear did not bring anxieties for safety

persian_text: آنکه بییسمع و بییبصر شده ست در حق آن بنده این هم بیهده ست
english_translation: who has become seeing by Me and hearing by Me this is foolish nonsense even in regard to that servant

persian_text: آنچنان کز فربهی هر یک جوان در نگنجیدی ز زفتی در جهان
english_translation: In such wise that each young man because of fatness was too stout to be contained in the world

persian_text: استن من عصمت و حفظ تو است جمله مطوی یمین آن دو دست
english_translation: Thy safeguarding and protection is my pillar  all is enfolded in the might of those two Hands

persian_text: اولا بشنو که چون ماندم ز شست اول و آخر ز پیش من بجست
english_translation: Firstly hear that when I abandoned net the first and the last this world and the next shot away from before me

persian_text: زان سبب که جمله اجزای منید جزو را از کل چرا بر میکنید
english_translation: Because ye all are parts of me Why will ye tear the part away from the whole

persian_text: ذم خورشید جهان ذم خودست که دو چشمم کور و تاریک به دست
english_translation: To blame the Sun of the world is to blame ones self for  My eyes are blind and dark and bad

persian_text: اوست دیوانه که دیوانه نشد این عسس را دید و در خانه نشد
english_translation: The madman is he that has not gone mad he that has seen this night patrol and has not gone home

persian_text: خوف حرمان ازل در کسب لوت چون نکردت سست اندر جست و جوت
english_translation: In earning food how has the fear of eternal disappointment not made you feeble in your search

persian_text: گوید او کین آفریدهی آن خداست که آفرینش بر خداییاش گواست
english_translation: He will reply that they were created by the Lord to whose Lordship the Creation bears witness

persian_text: تا فزاید قصر من بر آسمان که بدیدم قطب دوران زمان
english_translation: And that my palace may surpass heaven  saying I have seen the Pole on which Time revolves

persian_text: امرهم شوری برای این بود کز تشاور سهو و کژ کمتر رود
english_translation: their affair is consultation are to this purpose for owing to consultation mistakes and errors occur less

persian_text: گفت چون اقرار کردی پیش من رو که رستی تو ز آفات زمن
english_translation: He said Since thou hast made confession in my presence go  for thou art saved from the contaminations of Time

persian_text: ور ز غیر جنس باشد ذوق ما آن مگر مانند باشد جنس را
english_translation: And if our delight is from something not homogeneous that will surely resemble the congener

persian_text: نیل تمییز از خدا آموختست که گشاد آن را و این را سخت بست
english_translation: The Nile has learned from God to discriminate for it opened for these and shut fast against those

persian_text: او شناسد بوی میکاو میبخورد چون نخورد او میچه داند بوی کرد
english_translation: He knows the scent of the wine because he drank it  when he has not drunk it how can he scent it

persian_text: پیش حق آتش همیشه در قیام همچو عاشق روز و شب پیچان مدام
english_translation: Before God fire is always standing  writhing continually day and night like a lover

persian_text: جملگان چون اشتران بستهپای که گشایی زانوی ایشان برای
english_translation: All like tethered camels whose knees you unbind with foresight

persian_text: گر نبودی نسبت تو زین سرا پارهپاره کردمی این دم ترا
english_translation: Were it not for thy relationship to this house I would tear thee to pieces at this moment

persian_text: هوی هوی باد و شیر افشان ابر در غم مااند یک ساعت تو صبر
english_translation: The howling of the wind and the pouring forth of milk from the cloud are for care of us patience one moment

persian_text: صد حکایت بشنود مدهوش حرص در نیاید نکتهای در گوش حرص
english_translation: The man dazed by greed may hear a hundred stories not a single point comes into the ear of greed

persian_text: من چنان مردم که بر خونی خویش نوش لطف من نشد در قهر نیش
english_translation: I am such a man that the honey of my kindness did not become poison in wrath against my murderer

persian_text: صیقلی را بستهایای بینماز وآن هوا را کردهای دو دست باز
english_translation: Thou O prayerless man hast put the polisher in bonds and hast loosed the two hands of sensuality

persian_text: از تو میدزدیدمی وصف شتر جان من دید آن خود شد چشم پر
english_translation: I was stealing the camels description from thee my spirit saw its own camel it had its eye filled

persian_text: این جلالت در دلالت صادقست جمله ادراکات پس او سابقست
english_translation: This majesty in evidence declares the truth all perceptions are behind  He is outstripping

persian_text: گفت شه نیکوست خیر و موقعش لیک چون خیری کنی در موضعش
english_translation: The king replied Charity and the occasion for it are excellent  but when you perform an act of charity in its place

persian_text: انکار کردن موسی علیهالسلام بر مناجات شبان
english_translation: How Moses on whom be peace took offence at the prayer of the shepherd

persian_text: هست آهنگر بر آهن قیمی هست بنا هم بر آلت حاکمی
english_translation: The ironsmith is a superintendent of iron the builder also is a ruler over his tools

persian_text: زان بیابان عدم مشتاق شوق میرسند اندر شهادت جوق جوق
english_translation: Yearning with desire they come in troops from the Desert of Nonexistence into the visible world

persian_text: ژغژغ آن زان تحمل میکنی تا که خاموشانه بر مغزی زنی
english_translation: You endure the rattling of it in order that you may silently come into touch with a kernel

persian_text: طول و عرض و وصف قصه تو به تو در کلام آن بزرگ دین بجو
english_translation: Seek the length and breadth and all particulars of the story in the discourse of that prince of the Faith

persian_text: غنج و نازت مینگنجد در جهان باش تا که من شوم از تو جهان
english_translation: Thy coquetry and prideful airs are not contained in the world  wait till I spring up from thee

persian_text: قابل این گفتهها شو گوشوار تا که از زر سازمت من گوشوار
english_translation: Receive these words like the ear that I may make for you an earring of gold

persian_text: گفت اینک بین نشان پای دزد این طرف رفته ست دزد زن بمزد
english_translation: Look here said  See the thiefs footprints The pimping thief has gone this way  Look here said  See the thiefs footprints The thief whose wife is for hire has gone this way

persian_text: بوک از تاثیر جوی انگبین شهد گردد در تنم این زهر کین
english_translation: Perchance by the effect of the river of honey this poison of hatred may be turned into honey in my body

persian_text: زانک دنیا را همیبینند عین وآن جهانی را همیدانند دین
english_translation: Because they regard this world as ready money while they deem what concerns that world to be a debt

persian_text: کی گمان بردی که او عصیان کند آنک خوف جان فرعون آن کند
english_translation: How should he have thought that he would disobey and do that which the dread of Pharaohs soul

persian_text: تازیانه بر زدی اسبم بگشت گنبدی کرد و ز گردون بر گذشت
english_translation: Thou didst ply the lash and my horse shied made a bound and passed beyond the sky

persian_text: تا بنگشاید دری را دیدبان در درون هرگیز نجنبک این گمان
english_translation: Unless the Watcher open a door this idea never stirs within

persian_text: صد هزاران موی مکر و دمدمه چشم خوابانید آن دم ز ان همه
english_translation: Myriad hairs of deceit and fraud  and at that time he closed his eyes to it all

persian_text: گر محک داری گزین کن ور نه رو نزد دانا خویشتن را کن گرو
english_translation: If you have a touchstone choose otherwise go devote yourself to him that knows

persian_text: گبر میگوید میان آن سقر چه غمم بودی گرم کردی نظر
english_translation: Amidst Hellfire the infidel is saying What pain should I feel if He were to look on me

persian_text: چون فرود آیی ازین امرودبن کژ نماند فکرت و چشم و سخن
english_translation: When thou comest down from this peartree thy thoughts and eyes and words will no more be awry

persian_text: در قیامت بنده را گوید خدا هین چه کردی آنچ دادم من ترا
english_translation: At the Resurrection God will say to His servant Hark what have you done with that which I bestowed on you

persian_text: در حکایت گفتهایم احسان شاه در حق آن بینوای بیپناه
english_translation: We have related in the story the kindness shown by the King to that needy one who had no refuge

persian_text: گر نبندی دست او دست تو بستگر تو پایش نشکنی پایت شکست
english_translation: Unless you bind his hand he will bind yours unless you break his leg he will break yours

persian_text: پس بشر فرمود خود را مثلکم تا به جنس آیید و کم گردید گم
english_translation: Therefore he called himself a man like you that ye might come to your congener and might not become lost

persian_text: ای تو در اطباق قدرت منتهی منتهی ما در کمی و بیرهی
english_translation: O thou who hast reached the limit in the stages of Power we have reached the limit in failure and aberration

persian_text: نسل ایشان نیز هم بسیار شد نور احمد ناصر آمد یار شد
english_translation: Their offspring also multiplied the Light of Ahmad aided and befriended them

persian_text: ضرب دف و کف و نعرهی مرد و زن کرد پنهان نعرهی آن نعرهزن
english_translation: Tympana pulsata manus complosae virorum et feminarum clamores clamorem illius clamitantis celabant The pounding of tambourines hands and the loud shouts of men and women masked the screams of that screamer

persian_text: الوداعای دوستان من مردهام رخت بر چارم فلک بر بردهام
english_translation: Farewell O friends I am dead I have carried my belongings up to the Fourth Heaven

persian_text: ناقهی صالح چو جسم صالحان شد کمینی در هلاک طالحان
english_translation: The shecamel of Sálih became like the bodies of righteous men an ambush for the destruction of the wicked

persian_text: عادت خود را بگردانم به وقت این غبار از پیش بنشانم به وقت
english_translation: I alter My custom at the time  at the time I lay the dust in front

persian_text: گفتم ار سوی حقایق بشکفند چون ز اسم حرف رسمی واقفند
english_translation: I said  If they are open to the realities how are they acquainted with names of letters attached to the form

persian_text: ور تو پیغام خدا آری چو شهد که بیا سوی خداای نیکعهد
english_translation: But if you bring the honeylike message of God Come to God O thou who hast a good covenant

persian_text: آن ستارهی نحس هست اندر سما هم بدان سو بایدش کردن دوا
english_translation: the unlucky star is in the sky tis in that quarter it must be cured

persian_text: خاصه خورشید کمالی کان سری ست روز و شب کردار او روشنگری ست
english_translation: Especially the perfect Sun which is of yonder  day and night its action is illumination

persian_text: بر سر هر ریش جمع آمد مگس تا نبیند قبح ریش خویش کس
english_translation: Flies gather on every wound so that no one sees the foulness of his wound

persian_text: جز مگر مرغی که بد بیجان و پر یا چو ماهی گنگ بود از اصل کر
english_translation: Except maybe the bird that was without spirit or wings or was dumb and deaf like a fish from the beginning

persian_text: همچنان میرو ز هدهد تا عقاب ره نما والله اعلم بالصواب
english_translation: Even so proceed from the hoopoe to the eagle and show the way And God best knoweth the right course

persian_text: ما اشتهیت العقل مذ جننتنی ما حسدت الحسن مذ زینتنی
english_translation: I have never desired reason since thou madst me mad I have never envied beauty since thou didst adorn me

persian_text: چون ز نامردی دل آکنده بود ریش و سبلت موجب خنده بود
english_translation: When the heart is filled with unmanliness the beard and moustache are a cause of laughter

persian_text: او به حمام زنان دلاک بود در دغا و حیله بس چالاک بود
english_translation: He was a shampooer in the womens bath and very active in fraud and deceit

persian_text: این درازی مدت از تیزی صنع مینماید سرعت انگیزی صنع
english_translation: The swift motion produced by the action of God presents this length of duration as from the rapidity of Divine action

persian_text: اشک دیدهست از فراق تو دوان آه آه است از میان جان روان
english_translation: The tears of our eyes are running because of our separation from thee sigh after sigh is going from the midst of our souls

persian_text: گر به طبع این میکنی ای باد سرد گرد خط و دایرهی آن هود گرد
english_translation: If O icy wind thou art doing this by nature try to invade the line and circle drawn by Húd

persian_text: مرغ غافل میخورد دانه ز دام همچو اندر دام دنیا این عوام
english_translation: the heedless bird will eat grain from the trap as these common folk do in the trap of the present world

persian_text: راه کردی لیک در ظن چو برق عشر آن ره کن پی وحی چو شرق
english_translation: thou hast journeyed  but in opinion as lightning  make a tenth part of that journey for the sake of inspiration as the sunrise

persian_text: هین بیا بلقیس ورنه بد شود لشکرت خصمت شود مرتد شود
english_translation: Hark Bilqís come Else it will be bad  thy army will become thine enemy and will revolt

persian_text: گر چه آن صورت نگنجد در فلک نه به عرش و فرش و دریا و سمک
english_translation: Although that form is not contained in Heaven nor in the empyrean nor the earth nor the sea nor the Fish

persian_text: چون برآیم بر سرکوه بلند آخر عقبه ببینم هوشمند
english_translation: When I come up to the top of a high hill I regard attentively the end of the pass

persian_text: خرمن فرعون را داد او به باد هیچ شه را اینچنین صاحب مباد
english_translation: He gave Pharaohs stack to the wind  may no king have such a minister

persian_text: باز این مستی شهوت در جهان پیش مستی ملک دان مستهان
english_translation: Know again that this intoxication of lust in the world is deemed of small account beside the intoxication of the angels

persian_text: مسخی از تو صاحب خوبی شود یا بلیسی باز کروبی شود
english_translation: Through thee the deformed may become endowed with beauty or an Iblís may again become one of the Cherubim

persian_text: پیش مریم حاضر آید در نظر مادر یحیی که دور است از بصر
english_translation: To Mary the mother of Yahyá would appear present though she was far from her sight

persian_text: این خود آن نالهست کو کرد آشکار آنچ پنهانست یا رب زینهار
english_translation: This is only the wailful music that he has uttered that which is hidden  mercy O Lord

persian_text: هرکه دیدش در بخارا گفت خیز پیش از پیدا شدن منشین گریز
english_translation: Every one that saw him in Bukhárá said  Arise before showing thyself Do not sit  Flee

persian_text: چونک حرفی برنتابد این وصال واجب آید که کنم کوته مقال
english_translation: Since this union cannot bear a single letter it behoves me to cut short the discourse

persian_text: او بسوی سفل میراند فرسگر چه سوی علو جنباند جرس
english_translation: He gallops his horse towards lowness albeit he rings the bell aloft

persian_text: عاشق آیینه باشد روی خوب صیقل جان آمد و تقوی القلوب
english_translation: The beauteous face is in love with the mirror it is a polisher of the soul and of the fear of God in hearts

persian_text: بهر یزدان میزید نه بهر گنج بهر یزدان میمرد نه از خوف رنج
english_translation: He lives for Gods sake not for riches he dies for Gods sake not from fear of pain

persian_text: هرچه آید از جهان غیبوش در دلت ضیفست او را دار خوش
english_translation: Whatsoever comes into thy heart from the invisible world is they guest entertain it well

persian_text: لیک مقصود ازل تسلیم تستای مسلمان بایدت تسلیم جست
english_translation: Yet thy giving thyself up is the object of eternal purpose O Moslem thou must seek to give thyself up

persian_text: این حقیقت دان نه حقاند این همه نی بکلی گمرهانند این رمه
english_translation: Know the truth to be this all these are not in the right nor are this herd entirely astray

persian_text: همچنان کن زاهد اندر سال قحط بود او خندان و گریان جمله رهط
english_translation: Even as that ascetic was laughing in a year of drought while all folk were weeping

persian_text: صد هزاران طب جالینوس بود پیش عیسی و دمش افسوس بود
english_translation: Myriads were the therapeutic arts of Galen before Jesus and his breath they were a laughingstock

persian_text: هست سنی را یکی تسبیح خاص هست جبری را ضد آن در مناص
english_translation: The Sunní is unaware of the Jabrís glorification the Jabrí is unaffected by the Sunnís glorification

persian_text: سینه صیقلها زده در ذکر و فکر تا پذیرد آینهی دل نقش بکر
english_translation: they have polished their breasts in commemoration and meditation that the hearts mirror may receive the virgin image

persian_text: گر تو چیری و مرا خود یار نیست بنده فرمانم بدانم کار نیست
english_translation: If thou art powerful and I in sooth have no ally I am subject to His command I have nothing to do with that

persian_text: چون که اسرارت نهان در دل شود آن مرادت زودتر حاصل شود
english_translation: When your heart becomes the grave of your secret that desire of yours will be gained more quickly

persian_text: جمله تلوینها ز ساعت خاستست رست از تلوین که از ساعت برست
english_translation: All changes have arisen from the hours he that is freed from the hours is freed from change

persian_text: پیلبچه میخوری ای پارهخوار هم بر آرد خصم پیل از تو دمار
english_translation: O eater of bribes thou eatest the young elephant from thee too the Master of the elephant will wring the breath

persian_text: او بگفتی خانهی دل خلوتست خالی از کدیه مثال جنتست
english_translation: He would reply My hearts house is empty it is void of beggary like Paradise

persian_text: حرص نابیناست بیند مو بمو عیب خلقان و بگوید کو بکو
english_translation: The blind man is Greed he sees other peoples faults hair by hair and tells them from street to street

persian_text: گفت بیچون دیدم اما بهر قال بازگویم مختصر آن را مثال
english_translation: He replied My vision was ineffable but for the sake of argument I will briefly declare a parable thereof

persian_text: در شب دنیا که محجوبست شید ناظر حق بود و زو بودش امید
english_translation: In the night of this world where the sun is veiled he was beholding God and his hope was in Him

persian_text: گفت یزدان که به علم روشنم که ترا جلاد این خلقان کنم
english_translation: God said  by My resplendent knowledge I will make thee the executioner of these creatures

persian_text: لاجرم بسیارگو شد از نشاط مست ادب بگذاشت آمد در خباط
english_translation: Of course in consequence of glee he became loquacious the intoxicated man neglected respect and began to rave

persian_text: اصبعت در سیر پیدا میکند که نظر بر حرف داری مستند
english_translation: Thy finger in motion makes it evident that thou hast thine eye resting on the words

persian_text: جز خیالی را که دید آن اتفاق آنگهش بعدالعیان افتد فراق
english_translation: But only to the imagination that beholds it by chance and then after the vision is parted

persian_text: بانگ شیر و بانگ سگ در شب رسید صورت هر دو ز تاریکی ندید
english_translation: during the night the sound of a lion and the sound of a dog have come and he has not seen their forms on account of the darkness

persian_text: کای خدا تو منفقان را ده خلفای خدا تو ممسکان را ده تلف
english_translation: O God do Thou give the prodigal a boon in return and O God do Thou give the miserly a bane

persian_text: بارها من خواب دیدم مستمر که به بغدادست گنجی مستتر
english_translation: I have dreamed many times continuously that there is a concealed treasure at Baghdád

persian_text: بشکن آن شیشهی کبود و زرد را تا شناسی گرد را و مرد را
english_translation: Break those blue and yellow glasses in order that you may know the dust and the man

persian_text: جنگها کرده مظفر آمدند باز گشته با غنایم سودمند
english_translation: After the combat they came victorious they returned in possession of profit and with spoils

persian_text: همچو گربه باشد او بیدارهوش دزد در سوراخ ماند همچو موش
english_translation: It is mentally alert like a cat the thief remains in the hole like a mouse

persian_text: آتشی کاو باد دارد در بروت هم یکی بادی بر او خواند یموت
english_translation: The fire that has wind in its moustachea single puff of wind calls death upon it

persian_text: گر پدید آید به دعوی زینهار صورت او را مدارید اعتبار
english_translation: If he appear with the pretence  beware Do not have regard to his form

persian_text: دوستی تو و از تو ناشکفت حمدلله عاقبت دستم گرفت
english_translation: Praise be to God Thy friendship and not being able to do without thee have succoured me at last

persian_text: چشم بگذشته ازین محسوسها یافته از غیببینی بوسها
english_translation: The eye that has passed beyond these objects of senseperception and won kisses from vision of the Unseen

persian_text: ور نیاری خشک بر عجزی مهایست دانک با عاجز گزیده معجزیست
english_translation: And if thou canst not  do not stand inertly in a state of incapacity know that with incapable there is a goodly Incapacitator

persian_text: دم به دم در صفحهی اندیشهشان ثبت و محوی میکند آن بینشان
english_translation: Moment by moment that traceless One is setting down on the page of their thought and obliterating it

persian_text: او نیفتد در گمان از طعنشان او نگردد دردمند از ظعنشان
english_translation: He will not fall into opinion because of their taunts he will not be grieved by their departure

persian_text: در نبی شارکهم گفتست حق هم در اموال و در اولادای شفق
english_translation: O thou that glowest  God hath said in the Qurán Share with them in their wealth and children

persian_text: هین چه آوردید دستآویز را ارمغانی روز رستاخیز را
english_translation: Hark what have ye brought as an offeringa gift on homecoming for the Day when ye rise from the dead

persian_text: ژغژغ آن عقل و مغزت را برد صد هزاران عقل را یک نشمرد
english_translation: Their rattling robs you of understanding and brain and does not reckon myriads of understandings as one

persian_text: لعنت این باشد که سوزانت کند اوستاد جمله دزدانت کند
english_translation: This is Gods curse  that He makes you burn and makes you the master of all thieves

persian_text: کاغ کاغ و نعرهی زاغ سیاه دایما باشد به دنیا عمرخواه
english_translation: The cawing and noisy cry of the black crow is ever asking for life in this world

persian_text: مر ضعیفان را تو بیخصمی مدان از نبی ذا جاء نصر الله خوان
english_translation: Deem not the weak to be without a champion recite from the Qurán  When the help of God shall come

persian_text: یک کس نامستمع ز استیز و رد صد کس گوینده را عاجز کند
english_translation: A single person who obstinately refuses to listen will baffle a hundred speakers

persian_text: دست ظالم را ببر چه جای آنکه بدست او نهی حکم و عنان
english_translation: Cut off the wrongdoers hand what occasion is there for you to put the control and reins in his hand

persian_text: نه ترا حفظ زبان ز آزار کس نه نظر کردن به عبرت پیش و پس
english_translation: No holding thy tongue to avoid hurting any one no looking earnestly forward and backward

persian_text: حکایت آن زن پلیدکار کی شوهر را گفت کی آن خیالات از سر امرودبن مینماید ترا کی چنینها نماید چشم آدمی را سر آن امرودبن
english_translation: Story of the lewd woman who said to her husband Those illusions appear to thee from the top of the peartree for the top of that peartree causes the human eye to see such things come down from the top of the peartree

persian_text: وآنچنان که ساحران فرعون را رو سیه کردند از صبر و وفا
english_translation: And as the magicians blackened the face of Pharaoh by their fortitude and faithfulness

persian_text: نه چنان شیری که کس تیرش زند بل ز بیمش تیغ و پیکان بشکند
english_translation: Not such a lion as any one could shoot nay but by the terror of it sword and javelin would be shattered

persian_text: همچو حس آنک خواب او را ربود چون شد او بیدار عکسیت نمود
english_translation: Like the senseperceptions of one whom sleep has seized when he awakes the inverted quality becomes apparent

persian_text: ز اخروهن مرادش نفس تست کاو به آخر باید و عقلت نخست
english_translation: The thing meant by put them behind is your fleshly soul for it must be last and your intellect first

persian_text: گفت میگیرند کو خر جان عم چون نهای خر رو ترا زین چیست غم
english_translation: He replied O beloved of your uncle they are taking it because it is an ass since you are not an ass go  why are you troubled at this

persian_text: زادهی ثانیست احمد در جهان صد قیامت بود او اندر عیان
english_translation: Ahmad is the twiceborn in this world he was manifestly a hundred resurrections

persian_text: گرچه کشت این قوم را حق بارها ریخت بهر خونبها انبارها
english_translation: Though God hath killed these folk many a time He hath poured forth stores in payment of the bloodprice

persian_text: او روان است و تو گویی واقف است او دوان است و تو گویی عاکف است
english_translation: It is moving and you say it is standing it is running and you say it is keeping still

persian_text: اختلاف کردن در چگونگی و شکل پیل
english_translation: The disagreement as to the description and shape of the elephant

persian_text: خواندش در سورهی والنجم زود لیک آن فتنه بد از سوره نبود
english_translation: He recited it those words quickly in the Súra WalNajm but it was a temptation  it was not part of the Súra

persian_text: ابتدای کبر و کین از شهوت است راسخی شهوتت از عادت است
english_translation: The beginning of pride and hatred is in lust and the rootedness of your lust is from habit

persian_text: مر زنان را خلعت و صلت دهد کودکان را هم کلاه زر نهد
english_translation: He will give the women robes of honour and donations on the children too he will put mitres of gold

persian_text: اندر آن کاری که ثابت بودنیست قایمی ده نفس را که منثنیست
english_translation: Grant unto the soulfor it is bent to stand upright in the work wherein it ought to be stable

persian_text: زنده کرده و معتق و دربان تو این جهان و آن جهان بر خوان تو
english_translation: I am made living by thee and liberated by thee and am thy doorkeeper at thy table in this world and in the next

persian_text: گفت الهنا فی حوائجنا الیک والتمسناها وجدناها لدیک
english_translation: He said We have repaired for succour unto Thee in our needs and have sought them found them with Thee

persian_text: علت عاشق ز علتها جداست عشق اصطرلاب اسرار خداست
english_translation: The lovers ailment is separate from all other ailments love is the astrolabe of the mysteries of God

persian_text: نه چو معراج زمینی تا قمر بلک چون معراج کلکی تا شکر
english_translation: Tis not like the ascension of a piece of earth to the moon nay but like the ascension of a cane to sugar

persian_text: مزد حق کو مزد آن بیمایه کو این دهد گنجیت مزد و آن تسو
english_translation: What comparison is there between Gods reward and the reward given by that worthless creature The former gives you a treasure as your reward and the latter a groat

persian_text: در نمد پیچید و بر راهش نهاد معبر صدر جهان آنجا فتاد
english_translation: He wrapped him in the felt and laid him out on the road The Sadri Jahán happened to pass that way

persian_text: یک گره را بر هوا درهم زدی تا چو خشخاش استخوان ریزان شدی
english_translation: One party it hurled against each other in the air so that their bones crumbled like poppyseed

persian_text: مهربان بیرشوتان یاریگران در مقام سخت و در روز گران
english_translation: They are kind and bribeless ones helpers in the hard plight and the heavy day

persian_text: لا یسع فینا نبی مرسل و الملک و الروح ایضا فاعقلوا
english_translation: In Us is no room for a prophet sent as an apostle nor yet for the Angels or the Spirit Do ye therefore understand

persian_text: قرب آوازش گواهی میدهد کاین دم از نزدیک یاری میجهد
english_translation: The proximity of the voice gives him testimony that these words spring from a near friend

persian_text: که یقین دیدم درون تو شهی است کاو اولو العزم و رسول آگهی است
english_translation: I see with certainty within thee is a King who is possessed of firm purpose and is an Apostle endowed with knowledge

persian_text: بر جهید از جا و گفتا بخ لک آفتابی تاجر گشتت ای کلک
english_translation: She sprang up from her place and said Blessed art thou A sun has become a tiara for thee O poor bald man

persian_text: از نشاط از ما کرانه کردهای این تکبر از کجا آوردهای
english_translation: Because of exultation thou hast turned aside from us  whence hast thou brought this arrogance

persian_text: سر برهنه در سجود آنها که هیچ رویشان قبله ندید از پیچ پیچ
english_translation: Bareheaded in the prostrate attitude were those whose faces because of perversity had never seen the qibla at all

persian_text: گر چه صرصر بس درختان میکند با گیاهتر وی احسان میکند
english_translation: though the Sarsar wind uproots many trees it bestows kindness on the wet grass

persian_text: از صفاتش رستهای والله نخست در صفاتش باز رو چالاک و چست
english_translation: By God thou grewest from His attributes in the beginning go back nimbly and fleetly into His attributes

persian_text: بلک مرگش بیعنایت نیز نیست بیعنایت هان و هان جایی مهایست
english_translation: Nay not even his death is without the favour hark hark do not tarry anywhere without the favour

persian_text: میکند گستاخ مردم را به راه او بجان لرزانترست از برگ کاه
english_translation: He boldly directs people in the Way  he is more tremulous in soul than a blade of straw

persian_text: باز و اللیل است ستاری او و آن تن خاکی زنگاری او
english_translation: Again and by the night is his occultation and his earthen rustdark body

persian_text: ناتراشیده همیباید جذوع تا دروگر اصل سازد یا فروع
english_translation: Trunks of trees must be unhewn in order that the woodcutter may fashion the stem or the branches

persian_text: تا شوم من خاک پای آن کسی که به باغ لطف تستش مغرسی
english_translation: That I may become dust for the feet of the person who is planted in the orchard of thy favour

persian_text: گاه خورشید و گهی دریا شوی گاه کوه قاف و گه عنقا شوی
english_translation: Now thou becomest the Sun and now the Sea now the mountain of Qáf and now the Anqá

persian_text: شد عصا اندر کف موسی گوا شد عصا اندر کف ساحر هبا
english_translation: In the hand of Moses the rod became a witness  in the hand of the magician the rod became motes in the air

persian_text: گم شود چون بارگاه او رسید آب آمد مر تیمم را درید
english_translation: Will disappear now that his royal court has arrived the water is come and has torn up the ablution with sand

persian_text: تو یقین میدان که جرمی کردهای جبر را از جهل پیش آوردهای
english_translation: Know for sure that you have committed a sin and in folly have brought forward compulsion

persian_text: تا از آن واقف کنی مر عام را پخته گردانی بدین هر خام را
english_translation: That thou mayst acquaint the vulgar therewith and by this means make every raw person to become cooked

persian_text: خان و مان چغد ویرانست و بس نشنود اوصاف بغداد و طبس
english_translation: The wilderness alone is the house and goods of the owl he will not listen to descriptions of Baghdád and Tabas

persian_text: تو اندش پوشید هیچ از دیدهها وز طراوت دادن پوسیدهها
english_translation: Can he ever mask it from eyes and from giving freshness to things rotten

persian_text: چونک صبرت نیست زین آب سیاه چون صبوری داری از چشمهی اله
english_translation: Since thou hast not the patience to do without this black water how hast thou the patience to do without Gods fountain

persian_text: در فکن در نیلش و کن اعتماد من ترا با وی رسانم رو سپید
english_translation: Throw him into the Nile and put trust  I will bring thee to him happily

persian_text: غل بخل از دست و گردن دور کن بخت نو دریاب در چرخ کهن
english_translation: Take off the shackle of avarice from thy hands and neck seize a new fortune in the old heaven

persian_text: چون ندارم زور و ضبط خویشتن زین قضا و زین بلا و زین فتن
english_translation: Since I have not strength and selfcontrol from this destiny and this affliction and these tribulations

persian_text: زانک دوزخ گوید ای مومن تو زود برگذر که نورت آتش را ربود
english_translation: For Hell says O true believer pass by quickly since thy light hath taken away the Fire

persian_text: مبالغه کردن موش در لابه و زاری و وصلت جستن از چغز آبی
english_translation: How the mouse exerted himself to the utmost in supplication and humble entreaty and besought the waterfrog to grant him access

persian_text: خیر باشد نیمشب چه میکنی تو کیی گفتا دهلزنای سنی
english_translation: All is well I hope What are you doing at midnight Who are you He said A drummer O honourable sir

persian_text: که محمد گفت بر دست صبا از یمن میآیدم بوی خدا
english_translation: For Mohammed said The scent of God is coming to me from Yemen on the hand of the zephyr

persian_text: مرد مال و خلعت بسیار دید غره شد از شهر و فرزندان برید
english_translation: The man saw the much wealth and the many robes he was beguiled he parted from his town and children

persian_text: اشتر از چستی که با او شد روان موش غره شد که هستم پهلوان
english_translation: By reason of the readiness with which the camel set out along with him the mouse was duped into thinking himself a hero

persian_text: دو کس از اعیان آن ده یافتند در هلاک آن یکی بشتافتند
english_translation: They found two of the notables of that village and made haste to put one to death

persian_text: گفت ما گشتیم چون جان بند طین آفتاب جان توی در یوم دین
english_translation: He said We have been bound like the spirit in its prison of clay Thou art the Sun of the spirit on the Day of Judgement

persian_text: گفت روحم آن تو خود روح چیست هین بفرما کین تجشم بهر کیست
english_translation: He replied My spirit belongs to theewhat indeed is my spirit  Oh say on whose account is this solicitude

persian_text: چون که زد بسیار و گریان سر نهاد چنگ بالین کرد و بر گوری فتاد
english_translation: When he had played a long while and  weeping laid his head down he made the harp his pillow and dropped on a tomb

persian_text: هر چه بدهد نیم آن بدهم به تو همچنان کرد آن فقیر صلهجو
english_translation: I will pay thee half of whatever he may give The poor man desiring the present did just as he was told

persian_text: حکایت
english_translation: Story

persian_text: هست آن پندار او زیرا به راه صد هزاران پرده آمد تا اله
english_translation: It is his opinion because on the way to God there are a hundred thousand veils

persian_text: او نبیند غیر دستاری و ریش از معرف پرسد از بیش و کمیش
english_translation: He sees nothing but a turban and beard he asks the announcer about his merits and demerits

persian_text: حجت منکر هماره زردرو یک نشان بر صدق آن انکار کو
english_translation: The unbelievers argument is always shamefaced where is a single sign that indicates the truth of that unbelief

persian_text: بعد یکدم زهر بر جانش فتد زهر در جانش کند داد و ستد
english_translation: After one moment the poison falls on his spirit the poison exercises sway over his spirit

persian_text: ز آنکه مرگم همچو من خوش آمده ست مرگ من در بعث چنگ اندر زده ست
english_translation: Because death has become sweet as manna to me my death has laid fast hold of resurrection

persian_text: تو سزایی در همان رنج و بلا نعمت و اقبال کی سازد ترا
english_translation: Thou deservest to be in that trouble and affliction how should happiness and prosperity be suitable for thee

persian_text: یوسفان غیب لشکر میکشند تنگهای قند و شکر میکشند
english_translation: The Josephs of the unseen world are marching they are bringing bales of candy and sugar

persian_text: سیر گشته از ده و از روستا وز شکرریز چنان نا اوستا
english_translation: They became sick of the village and the country and of the sugared expressions of such an uninstructed boor

persian_text: تسلیم کردن مرد خود را به آن چه التماس زن بود ولی در طبع هر دانندهای هست که با گردنده گردانندهای هست
english_translation: How the man yielded to his wifes request To the mind of every knowing man it is a fact that with the revolving object there is one that causes it to revolve

persian_text: گفت نایب یک به یک ما بادییم با سواد وجه اندر شادییم
english_translation: The deputy replied We are aggressors every one of us but notwithstanding our blackness of face we are happy

persian_text: یک نشانی که بخندد پیش تو یک نشان که دست بندد پیش تو
english_translation: One sign is that he will smile before you one sign is that he will fold his hands in your presence

persian_text: در سر خود پیچ هل خیرهسری رو در دل زن چرا بر هر دری
english_translation: Attend to thine own head abandon giddyheadedness go knock at the door of thy heart why art thou at every door

persian_text: ای لطیفی که گل سرخت بدید از خجالت پیرهن را بر درید
english_translation: O Lovely One whom the red rose beheld and tore its shirt in shame

persian_text: دست بر نبضش نهاد و یک به یک باز میپرسید از جور فلک
english_translation: He laid his hand on her pulse and put questions one by one about the injustice of Heaven

persian_text: آن گروهی کز ادب بگریختند آب مردی و آب مردان ریختند
english_translation: The folk who fled from correction dishonoured their manhood and men

persian_text: دام دیگر بد که عقلش در نیافت وحی غایببین بدین سو زان شتافت
english_translation: there was another trap which reason did not perceive hence the inspiration which beholds the unseen sped in this direction

persian_text: ور سؤالت را بسی فاییدههاست پس جهان بیفایده آخر چراست
english_translation: And if there are many profits in your question then why pray is the world unprofitable

persian_text: مرغ بیهنگامی ای بدبخت رو ترک ما گو خون ما اندر مشو
english_translation: Thou art an untimely bird Begone O miserable one take leave of us do not wade in our blood

persian_text: بحر را گویم که هین پر نار شو گویم آتش را که رو گلزار شو
english_translation: I say to the sea Hark be full of fire I say to the fire Go be a rosegarden

persian_text: گر ببرد او به قهر خود سرم شاه بخشد شصت جان دیگرم
english_translation: The king if he behead me in his wrath will bestow on me sixty other lives

persian_text: همچو ابلیسی که گفت اغویتنی تو شکستی جام و ما را میزنی
english_translation: Like Iblís who said Thou hast led me astray Thou hast broken the cup and art beating me

persian_text: مختصر کردم چو آمد ده پدید خود نبود آن ده ره دیگر گزید
english_translation: I will abridge When the village came in sight it was not in sooth that village  he chose another road

persian_text: عقلها زین سر بود بیرون در زهرهی وهم ار بدرد گو بدر
english_translation: Intellects are excluded from this mystery if the heart of Imagination burst let it burst

persian_text: گرچه پلهی چشم بر هم میزنی در سفینه خفتهای ره میکنی
english_translation: Albeit thou art closing thine eyelids thou art asleep in the ship and voyaging

persian_text: صورت آواز مرغست آن کلام غافلست از حال مرغان مرد خام
english_translation: That terminology is the image of the birds voice the uninitiated man is ignorant of the state of the birds

persian_text: مصطفی فرمود اگر گویم به راست شرح آن دشمن که در جان شماست
english_translation: Mustafá said If I should tell aright the description of the enemy which is in your souls

persian_text: آب بگذارید و نان قسمت کنید بخل بگذارید اگر آل منید
english_translation: What are water and vinegar Deal out bread  discard avarice if ye are my people

persian_text: ور نرفتی وز ستیزه شستهای تو به معنی رفتهای بگسستهای
english_translation: And if thou goest not but remainest sitting in despite  thou art gone in reality and severed

persian_text: شاه هم بر خوی خود گفتش هزار چون چنین بد عادت آن شهریار
english_translation: The Sháh indeed according to his habit ordered a thousand to him since such was the custom of that monarch

persian_text: رو نمایم صبح بهر گوشمال تا نگردید از منی ز اهل شمال
english_translation: At dawn I show my face to reprimand you lest from egoism ye become among the people of the left hand

persian_text: نقش کژمژ دیدم اندر آب و گل چون ملایک اعتراضی کرد دل
english_translation: I have seen the crooked misshapen image in water and clay and like the angels my heart has raised an objection

persian_text: پاس دارد قلعه را از دشمنان قلعه نفروشد به مال بیکران
english_translation: Guards the fortress from enemies and will not sell it for boundless riches

persian_text: تا نگردد فوت این نوع التقا کان نظر بختست و اکسیر بقا
english_translation: In order that this kind of meeting should not be lost for to be looked on by them is fortune and an elixir of immortality

persian_text: ور بخسبد هست چون اصحاب کهف صوفیان کردند پیش شیخ زحف
english_translation: And if he sleep he is like the Men of the Cave did the Súfís march to war before the Shaykh

persian_text: بعد از آن صبرش نماند و آن دگر بر گشاد و کرد خرج آن قمر
english_translation: Afterwards she could no longer restrain herself and she opened the other and spent it on that Moon

persian_text: معنی نختم علی افواههم این شناس اینست رهرو را مهم
english_translation: Know that this is the meaning of We seal their mouths this is important for the traveller on the Way

persian_text: گفت نی خامش رها کنهای هو نسبت و نام قدیمت را بگو
english_translation: Nay said he hush cease from outcry tell thy ancient lineage and name

persian_text: که عمادالملک بد پای علم بهر هر مظلوم و هر مقتول غم
english_translation: For the Imádu lMulk was the foot of the banner to which every victim of injustice and every one stricken by distress would flock for refuge

persian_text: گبر این بشنید و نوری شد پدید در دل او تا که زناری برید
english_translation: The fireworshipper heard this and a light appeared in his heart so that he cut a girdle

persian_text: چون شیاطین با غلیظیهای خویش واقفاند از سر ما و فکر و کیش
english_translation: Since the devils notwithstanding their grossnesses are acquainted with our inmost soul and thought and belief

persian_text: بس که میافتاد از پری ثمار تنگ میشد معبر ره بر گذار
english_translation: Inasmuch as the fruit was falling from abundance there was no room for any one to pass on the road

persian_text: شکر حق را کان دعا مردود شد من زیان پنداشتم آن سود شد
english_translation: Thanks to God that that prayer was rejected I thought twas loss but it has turned out to be gain

persian_text: تیغ حلمت جان ما را چاک کرد آب علمت خاک ما را پاک کرد
english_translation: The sword of thy forbearance hath rent my soul the water of thy knowledge hath purified my earth

persian_text: اجابت کردن حقتعالی دعای موسی را علیهالسلام
english_translation: How God most High answered favourably the prayer of Moses on whom be peace

persian_text: دید زاید از یقین بی امتهال آنچنانک از ظن میزاید خیال
english_translation: Vision is immediately born of certainty just as fancy is born of opinion

persian_text: وین مراعاتش یکی صفعی دگر وین ز صفع آشکارا سختتر
english_translation: And this respect was to him another slap and it was harder than the visible slap

persian_text: آسیای چرخ بر بیگندمان موسپیدی بخشد و ضعف میان
